definition.en: Action is more like growth than force. It is a by-product of any reality, and a part of all reality.
definition.zh: 行动更像成长而非力量。它是任何实相的副产品，也是所有实相的一部分。
所有行动都是同时性的。

definition.en: Action is not a function of structure. Action is inseparable from structure. Structure is action.
definition.zh: 行动并非结构的功能。行动与结构不可分割。结构就是行动。

definition.en: Every action affects every other action.
definition.zh: 每个行动都影响每个其他行动。

explanation:
行动的本质更接近“生长”而非“力量”。它是所有现实的副产品，也是现实本身的一部分。行动远不止你理解的“移动”，价值实现本身就是行动。梦境也是行动——不仅是梦中的动作，做梦这个行为本身也是行动。行动之中永远嵌套着行动，任何现实或体验都是瞬间的行动。你熟悉的“运动”只是行动在物理场中的一种形式，因为你的外在感官才显得重要，大量行动完全不被你有意识地感知。

行动本身是一种不平衡，它无法被当作单一事物感知，而是一种关系和维度。在梦境中，行动最接近其本来面目，因为自我放松了限制。自我试图将行动视为与自身分离的客体，甚至认为行动由自我发起——但这只是幻觉，因为自我本身就是行动。人格是同时性的行动，

quotes.text: Action is more like growth than force. It is a by-product of any reality, and a part of all reality. You should be able to see many implications here when you read this material over. Again, action involves more than movement, as you think of movement, for value fulfillment is action. A dream involves action. Not only the action within the dream, but the action of dreaming itself. You may here get a glimmering of the connection between certain types of action and distance, as I have mentioned it briefly. There is always action within action, and any reality or any experience is instantaneous action. Motion is the type of action with which you are most familiar, but motion attains its importance within the physical field only because of your particular outer senses. For much action is entirely unperceived by you, particularly on a conscious level.
quotes.translation: 行动更像成长而非力量。它是任何实相的副产品，也是所有实相的一部分。当你重读这份资料时，应该能从中看出许多含义。再次强调，行动不仅包含你所以为的运动，因为价值完成就是行动。梦境包含行动——不仅是梦中的行动，更是做梦这个行为本身。在此你或许能隐约窥见某些类型的行动与距离之间的关联，正如我先前简要提及的。行动之中永远有行动，任何实相或经验都是瞬间行动。运动是你最熟悉的行动类型，但运动之所以在物质场域中显得重要，完全是因为你特定的外在感官。因为许多行动完全不被你感知，尤其在意识层面。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: There is always an imbalance here that may of itself be termed action. It cannot be perceived as any one thing, for it is a relationship and a dimension. It can be perceived most directly, and with less distortion, in the dream state. For here it is allowed the freedom of itself. Here you have also the inner vitality that has not been materialized within the realm of material camouflage. You have the tendency of this inner vitality to materialize, and its inability to completely do so. In the dream state the tendency for this vitality to materialize meets with little resistance. Action within actions result, without physical space. Distances appear and are experienced as such. Action is much less limited. Rather, action itself is not less limited, but you can experience action with less limitations, for the ego which erects such limitations lets down its guard.
quotes.translation: 这里始终存在一种不平衡，其本身即可称为行动。它无法被感知为单一事物，因为它是一种关系与维度。在梦境状态中，它能以最少扭曲被最直接地感知。因为在此它得以享有自身的自由。这里你还能找到尚未在物质伪装领域物质化的内在活力。你拥有这种内在活力想要物质化的倾向，以及它无法完全物质化的特性。在梦境状态中，这种活力物质化的倾向几乎遇不到阻力。行动中的行动由此产生，无需物理空间。距离显现并被如实体验。行动所受的限制大为减少。更准确地说，并非行动本身限制减少，而是你得以在较少限制下体验行动，因为设立这些限制的自我放下了戒备。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: But ego's seeming independence from action is basically meaningless, since ego is also action, and can never be otherwise. Any such separation of action from itself only adds to the totality of action, in that it increases actions ability to perceive itself from as many viewpoints as possible. Perspectives represent action's action upon itself. Any one dimension must result in another dimension, for the action within any given dimension can never complete itself, but will continue.
quotes.translation: 但自我看似独立于行动的状态，本质上毫无意义，因为自我本身就是行动，且永远不可能不是。任何将行动与自身分离的行为，只会增加行动的整体性——因为这增加了行动从尽可能多的视角感知自身的能力。视角代表行动对自身的作用。任何维度必然导致另一维度的产生，因为任何既定维度中的行动永远无法自我完成，而会持续延续。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Consciousness of self involves a consciousness of self within, amid, and as a part of action. ego consciousness, on the other hand, involves a state in which consciousness of self attempts to divorce itself from action, an attempt on the part of consciousness to perceive action as an object. Here we see that ego consciousness, in this attempt, strives to perceive action not only as separate, but to perceive it in such a fashion that it appears to ego that action is not only separate from itself, that is separate from the ego, but that action is initiated by the ego, and a result rather than a cause of ego's own existence.
quotes.translation: 自我意识包含一种在行动之内、作为行动一部分的自我意识。而自我意识则涉及一种状态，其中自我意识试图与行动分离，这是意识试图将行动视为客体的尝试。在此我们看到，自我意识在这种尝试中，不仅力求将行动视为分离的，而且以这样一种方式感知它，使得自我看来行动不仅与自身分离——即与自我分离——而且行动是由自我发起的，是自我自身存在的结果而非原因。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The personality structure can be studied from many viewpoints. We are now studying it in relation to its basic reality as action. While it may seem that the personality would be a result of a series of actions, this is not basically the case. The personality in actuality is simultaneous action, that is composed of actions within actions. Portions of it are conscious of its awareness as a part of action, and portions of it attempt to stand aside from action.
quotes.translation: 人格结构可以从许多角度研究。现在我们正研究它与其作为行动的基本实相的关系。虽然人格似乎是系列行动的结果，但基本上并非如此。人格实际上是同时性的行动，由行动中的行动组成。其部分意识到自身作为行动的一部分，而部分则试图置身于行动之外。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: You say that an action has begun when it enters your system. You say that an action is completed when it passes beyond your system. But the action, in those terms, began long before; and the action, in those terms, is never completed. Yet even the action as it occurs within your system is constantly changing in the ways which we have described, and this changing in itself implies infinities of beginnings and endings within action itself, with no ending, a real or permanent ending, and no beginning, a real beginning out of nothing. For each beginning carries within it action which has come before.
quotes.translation: 当行动进入你的系统时，你说它开始了。当行动超越你的系统时，你说它完成了。但就这些术语而言，行动早在很久以前就开始了；而就这些术语而言，行动从未完成。然而，即使行动发生在你的系统内，也以我们描述的方式不断变化，这种变化本身意味着行动内部有无穷的开端和终结，没有真正的或永久的终结，也没有从虚无中产生的真正开端。因为每个开端都承载着先前发生的行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Action can be experienced directly, however, but only when no effort is made to tamper with it. It must be plunged into. Once more, action is not a function of structure. Action is inseparable from structure. Structure is action. Identities are action, as I have explained. Your idea of action as it occurs within dreams comes closer to the real nature of action than does your idea of muscular force. For in dreams the ego makes little attempt to impede action. Though in dreams you see or feel your arm move, your legs run, still the arm and the legs of the physical body may not move.
quotes.translation: 然而，只有当你不试图干预行动时，才能直接体验它。你必须纵身跃入其中。再次强调，行动并非结构的功能。行动与结构不可分割。结构即是行动。身份认同即是行动，正如我先前解释过的。你们对行动在梦境中发生的认知，比你们对肌肉力量的认知更接近行动的真实本质。因为在梦境中，自我几乎不会试图阻碍行动。尽管在梦中你看见或感觉到手臂移动、双腿奔跑，但肉体的手臂和双腿可能纹丝不动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Every action affects every other action. Mental enclosures are energy units possessing all the characteristics of such units as I have previously given you. You know that all action is basically mental action, as fields replenish each other. As no action is meaningless, so the dream universe and the physical universe constantly replenish each other, and mental actions are performed in both universes, being camouflage acting symbols for inner action.
quotes.translation: 每个行动都会影响其他所有行动。心灵围场是能量单元，具备我之前给予你们的这类单元的所有特性。你们知道所有行动本质上都是心灵行动，如同场域相互补给。既然没有行动是无意义的，那么梦境宇宙与物质宇宙便不断相互补给，心灵行动在两个宇宙中同时进行，作为内在行动的象征性伪装符号。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: I will have more to say concerning the connection between the two fields and their manifestation in action, for one of the closest glimpses you can get of pure action is action as it is involved within the dream universe, and in this mobility as the personality passes into and out of the dream field. Within your own universe you deal with the transformation of action into physical manipulations, but this involves but a small portion of the nature of action, and it is my purpose to familiarize you with action as it exists more or less in its pure form.
quotes.translation: 关于这两个场域及其在行动中的显化之间的关联，我还会进一步阐述，因为你们能最接近纯粹行动的方式之一，便是观察行动在梦境宇宙中的运作，以及人格进出梦境场域时的这种流动性。在你们自己的宇宙中，你们处理的是行动转化为物质操作的过程，但这仅涉及行动本质的一小部分，而我的目的是让你们熟悉行动或多或少以其纯粹形式存在的样貌。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Action may show itself as motion, but it is much more than motion in the terms which you usually use, and motion is but one small dimension within actions realm. All types of consciousness represent a different focus of energy's perception within itself. There is no past or future to action. All action is simultaneous. Identities, some identities and some forms of consciousness, particularly the ego, perceive a past or a present, but this is merely the result of the manner in which such identities and consciousness view available data.
quotes.translation: 行动可能以运动的形式显现，但它远不止于你们通常理解的运动范畴，运动只是行动领域中的一个微小维度。所有类型的意识都代表着能量在其自身内部感知的不同焦点。行动没有过去或未来之分，所有行动都是同时发生的。某些身份和意识形态，特别是自我，会感知到过去或现在，但这仅仅是这些身份和意识看待可用数据的方式所导致的结果。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Here is an analogy. Imagine then the inner vitality being some cosmic sphere, but a sphere of more dimensions than you can imagine. Its motion could be called action, but this is deceptive because action is the basic nature of the sphere itself. Action is its composition. Action is that from which it is, therefore it moves, it acts outward. But all outwardness turns ultimately inward, and then again outward in all directions. And each inward action forms a new dimension that must, again, be thrust outward toward utilization.
quotes.translation: 这里打个比方。想象内在活力是一个宇宙球体，但其维度远超你们的想象。它的运动可称为行动，但这具有误导性，因为行动是球体本身的基本属性。行动就是它的构成。行动是它存在的本质，因此它运动，它向外作用。但所有向外最终都会转向向内，然后再度向各个方向向外。每个向内行动都会形成一个新的维度，这个维度必须再次被推向向外以实现其功用。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: These secondary personalities, known to psychologists, have a much more important place within reality than is suspected. No psychic action is invalid. Every psychic action exists, has an effect and has durability in terms of value fulfillment. Every psychic action, and a psychic action is any psychic happening such as a dream, or thought, that may have no existence in terms of space and time, every psychic action then contains within it the potentialities of value fulfillment, transference, and even energy transformation.
quotes.translation: 心理学家所知的这些次级人格，在实相中占据着比人们所猜想的更重要的位置。没有哪种心灵行动是无效的。每个心灵行动都存在、产生作用，并在价值完成方面具有持久性。每个心灵行动——所谓心灵行动是指任何可能不存在于时空中的心灵事件，如梦境或思想——每个心灵行动都内含着价值完成、转移甚至能量转化的潜能。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: It is therefore obvious why one action affects all others, so intimately that it is basically impossible to speak of one action in isolation. Tension is a condition of action, and an inherent quality of action. The possibilities of action are limitless. Regardless of the origin of any given action, it will never be entirely dissipated. It may pass beyond or through the system in which it originated, but its existence will not cease.
quotes.translation: 因此显而易见，为何一个行动会如此密切地影响所有其他行动，以至于基本上不可能孤立地谈论一个行动。张力是行动的一种状态，也是行动的内在特质。行动的可能性是无限的。无论任何特定行动的起源为何，它永远不会完全消散。它可能超越或穿过它起源的系统，但其存在不会终止。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The longer the impeding action is accepted as a part of the self, the more serious the problem. The impeding action or illness however is not a part of the basic personality structure, or action gestalt, which is composed of action patterns formed since birth. Compared to this truly astounding structure, that is the result of the memory of every atom and molecule, this impeding action is relatively unimportant, and when correct methods are used, it can be dislodged without too much difficulty.
quotes.translation: 阻碍性行动被接受为自我一部分的时间越长，问题就越严重。然而，阻碍性行动或疾病并非基本人格结构或行动完形的一部分，后者由出生以来形成的行动模式构成。与这个真正惊人的结构——它是每个原子和分子记忆的结果——相比，这个阻碍性行动相对不重要，当使用正确方法时，可以不太困难地将其移除。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: You conceive of action in terms of time, since within the physical field a given action appears to actually take up time, almost in the same way that a chair seems to take up space. The chair of course does not take up space, but is part of what you call space. Nor does the action take up time. It is part of what you call time. Nevertheless, in the electrical system there is distance in terms of action. Each action is separate and not continuous with other action, in terms of continuity.
quotes.translation: 你们以时间的概念来理解行动，因为在物质场域中，一个特定行动似乎确实占据时间，几乎就像椅子似乎占据空间一样。当然，椅子并不占据空间，而是你们所谓空间的一部分。行动也不占据时间，它是你们所谓时间的一部分。然而，在电性系统中，存在以行动衡量的距离。就连续性而言，每个行动都是独立的，不与其他行动连续。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now. Moment points are indeed composed of action, action experiencing itself. What you see and experience physically represents but a small portion, a mere fraction of what reality is. There are universes within universes. Action is, and yet forms its own experience. Thoughts take electromagnetic patterns that have their own materializations as universal systems. Your physical body is a series of actions, though the word series is being used for simplicity's sake only.
quotes.translation: 现在。瞬间点确实由行动构成，行动体验着它自身。你们在物质层面所见所体验的，只是实相的一小部分，不过是冰山一角。宇宙之中还有宇宙。行动存在，却又形成它自身的体验。思想具有电磁模式，这些模式作为宇宙系统拥有自己的物质化显现。你们的物质身体是一系列行动，虽然"系列"一词仅为简便起见而使用。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

source_raw.title: (We received a letter from Pat Norelli today in which she substantiates some clairvoyant material concerning her, given by Seth in Session 340. Notes are included in that session.)

quotes.text: Identities may be termed action which is conscious of itself. For the purposes of our discussion, the terms action and identity must be separated. However basically no such separation exists, for an identity is also a dimension of existence, action within action, an unfolding of action upon itself; and through this interweaving of action with itself, through this reaction, an identity is formed.
quotes.translation: 本体可被称为对自身有意识的行动。为便于讨论，我们必须将行动与本体这两个术语加以区分。然而根本上并不存在这种分离，因为本体也是存在的一个维度，是行动中的行动，是行动对自身的展开；通过行动与自身的交织，通过这种反作用，本体得以形成。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Action is not affected by time as you know it. Action also takes place within the spacious present. You may, however, only perceive parts of action in your time breakdown. Ideally, psychological time experiences will allow you to perceive action more clearly and directly. The ego attempts to control action by standing apart from it. Any such division is arbitrary, and in no way affects the nature of action itself. All that changes is your perception of it.
quotes.translation: 行动不受你们所认知的时间影响。行动也发生在广袤的当下之中。不过，你们只能以时间分解的方式感知到行动的部分。理想情况下，心理时间体验将使你们更清晰直接地感知行动。自我试图通过置身事外来控制行动。任何此类划分都是武断的，丝毫不会影响行动本身的本质。唯一改变的是你们对它的感知。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now for a moment we will return to our material on action, and you may perhaps see why this fits in so well here. No action is identical to any other action. An action is never entirely dissipated, though it may pass beyond its particular field of origin. This transference, incidentally, from one system to another, necessarily changes the action itself; but for simplicity's sake we may say that an action has its reality within many systems simultaneously.
quotes.translation: 现在我们暂且回到关于'行动'的素材，你或许能明白为何此处如此契合。没有任何行动与其他行动完全相同。行动永远不会完全消散，尽管它可能超越其特定的起源领域。这种从一个系统到另一个系统的转移，必然改变行动本身；但为简化起见，我们可以说一个行动同时存在于多个系统中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Once such a pathway has been constructed, we have what you may call an action pattern or habit. Therefore, when cross currents of action are constructed, action will continue in those directions unless it is diverted back to other channels. Then the secondary or impeding channel will automatically be closed off. But all action must be withdrawn from it, for as long as the channel remains, then the possibility remains that the impeding action will reoccur.
quotes.translation: 一旦这样的路径被构建，我们就有了所谓的行动模式或习惯。因此，当行动的交叉流被构建时，行动会持续朝这些方向前进，除非它被引导回其他渠道。随后次要或阻碍性的渠道会自动关闭。但所有行动都必须从中撤回，因为只要渠道存在，阻碍性行动重现的可能性就依然存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

source_raw.title: （In Volume I of The Early Sessions this sensation, as I call it, is dealt with following Session 23, in Session 24, and following Session 25, among others. I have experienced it frequently during psychological time since then. It is much richer and more varied than the more usual thrilling one notes upon a moving occasion. In the above instance, the initial source of the sensation appeared to stem from outside the body, and the waves against my ears had a tangible, solid quality that is difficult to describe.

quotes.text: We will also be concerned with the framework of the human personality as it exists within several levels of reality. We will conclude our discussion concerning the nature of action, in so far as action is considered by itself. We will always be concerned with a discussion of action, even though other subjects are under consideration however, since it is impossible to divide action into various parts even for the purpose of discussion.
quotes.translation: 我们也将关注存在于多个实相层面中的人类人格架构。我们将结束关于行动本质的讨论——就行动本身而言。然而即便在探讨其他主题时，我们始终会涉及行动的讨论，因为即使为了讨论之便，也无法将行动分割成不同部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The thought, as an electrical reality, is not subject to the physical laws that bind the physical body. The durability of the thought as an electrical act is determined by its initial electric charge. This electric charge can propel the thought, as an electrical action, through the apparent dimensions of your physical time in such a manner that it can exist simultaneously within your past and your present. Because a thought, as electric action, may exist both within your past and your present, this is not to say that it becomes two electric actions, one existing in the past and one existing in the future. Rather the one electric action or thought is simultaneously projected, through a peculiarity of its axis, so that it appears within your field, not in two places at once, but in two times at once.
quotes.translation: 思想作为电性实相，不受束缚物质身体的物理法则所限。思想作为电性行为的持久性，取决于其初始电荷。这种电荷能使思想作为电性行动，穿透你们物理时间的显见维度，以同时存在于过去与现在的方式运作。由于思想作为电性行动可能同时存在于过去与现在，这并非意味着它分裂为两个电性行动——一个存在于过去，另一个存在于未来。而是单一的电性行动或思想，通过其轴线的特殊性质被同时投射，使它在你们场域中显现时，并非同时出现在两个空间位置，而是同时出现在两个时间点。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: A certain portion of physical growth, in terms of a series of physical moments, is therefore necessary for value fulfillment to show itself within a physical organism. Within the dream field and within many other systems, this series of moments is unknown. Development comes not from a series of actions strung out along a single line, one before the other in lengthwise fashion. Instead development is largely a matter of value fulfillment, which is achieved through the perspectives of action, through traveling within any given action, and following it and changing with it. To make this clearer, I have said that action exists within limitless perspectives, and that you are mainly familiar with it as it is materialized along a single line of continuity within the physical system. You experience action then as if you were moving along a single line, each dot on the line representing a moment of your time. But at the imaginary point on your line that represents any given moment, action moves out in all directions. From the standpoint of that moment point, you could imagine action forming an imaginary circle with that point as an apex. But this happens at the point of every moment.
quotes.translation: 就一系列物理时刻而言，物质成长的特定阶段，是价值实现得以在物质有机体内显现的必要条件。在梦境场域及许多其他系统中，这种时刻序列并不存在。发展并非来自沿单一线条纵向排列的前后相继行动序列，而主要是价值实现的结果——通过行动的视角，在特定行动中穿行、追随并与之共变而达成。为阐明这点，我曾说过行动存在于无限视角中，而你们主要熟悉的是它在物质系统内沿单一连续性线条物质化的样貌。因此你们体验行动时，仿佛沿着单一线条移动，线条上的每个点代表你们时间的一个瞬间。但在代表任何给定时刻的想象点上，行动会向所有方向辐射。从该时刻点的立场来看，你们可以想象行动以该点为顶点形成虚拟圆环——而每个时刻点都在发生这种过程。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: I mentioned earlier that the personality is an excellent example of action as it is sometimes projected into the physical field, while not appearing within it in tangible form. For here we see many of actions characteristics: the mobility that does not necessarily involve space, the thrusts outward, and the corresponding thrusts inward. We see action acting upon itself and constant change. A thorough study of the personality will be an excellent exercise, and will lead you close to the nature of action itself. The experiences about which you asked, for example, involved actions of the personality and not, of course, of the ego. Expansion and contraction occur constantly as characteristic of action, as I have explained.
quotes.translation: 我先前提到，人格是行动投射入物质场域却未以有形方式显现的绝佳范例。因为在此我们可见行动的诸多特性：不必然涉及空间的流动性、向外推进与相应的向内推进。我们看见行动作用于自身与持续变化。对人格的透彻研究将是绝佳练习，能使你们接近行动本身的本质。例如你们所问及的经验，涉及的是人格的行动（当然并非自我）。正如我所解释的，扩张与收缩作为行动的特性持续发生。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: By slowing down his perception of action, man imagines that he lengthens time. This of course is not the case. He merely succeeds in perceiving action as bits and pieces, and fights its flow. On the one hand action is indeed simultaneous, yet in it all action is contained, for it occurs within the unlimited spacious present. In dreams action is given more freedom, and allowed to flow in a less hampered fashion.
quotes.translation: 当人类放慢对行动的感知时，便以为自己延长了时间。这当然并非事实。他只是成功地将行动感知为支离破碎的片段，并抗拒其流动。一方面，行动确实是同时发生的，然而所有行动都包含其中，因为它发生在无限的广阔当下。在梦境中，行动被赋予更多自由，得以以更不受阻碍的方式流动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The mental enzymes within the enclosure are the elements that set off the action and--listen closely--are also the action itself. In other words the mental enzymes not only produce action in the material world but become the action. I will always call any materialization an action from here on in, since as you both know by now nothing is stationary. If you will read over the above three or four paragraphs you will come close to seeing where mental and physical become one.
quotes.translation: 围场内的精神酵素是触发行动的元素——请仔细聆听——同时也是行动本身。换言之，精神酵素不仅在物质世界中产生行动，更成为行动本身。从此刻起，我将始终称任何物质化为行动，因为你们俩现在都明白，没有任何事物是静止的。若你们重读上述三四段文字，便会接近看见精神与物质合而为一之处。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Now, involving the performance on Ruburts part this evening, we have here once more another example of the nature in which action is changed by itself. For indeed, as it was possible for Ruburt to some slight degree to allow her friend to speak, nevertheless the action involved in the whole situation nevertheless changed not only Ruburt, but also necessarily changed her Father Trainor, in that any action of its own nature can never remain the same. His personality, that is the Father Trainor personality, was of necessity changed by this communication in the same manner that any experience will always change any personality.
quotes.translation: 现在，关于鲁柏今晚的表现，我们在此又获得另一个例证，说明行动如何被自身改变。诚然，尽管鲁柏能稍微允许她的朋友发言，但整个情境所涉及的行动不仅改变了鲁柏，也必然改变了她的父亲特雷纳——因为任何行动就其本质而言，永远无法保持原状。他的人格，即父亲特雷纳的人格，必然因这次沟通而改变，正如任何经验永远会改变任何人格一样。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: These directions are not forced upon action by any laws. They are merely the resultant patterns with which energy expresses itself. You are only familiar as a rule with this impetus, or these directions, in rather shallow manners, for the ego prefers not to perceive them. These motions are merely the flow which action takes. What you term negative suggestions are usually impeding actions, or directions of action which impede the main directive inner flow. They operate then in much the same manner as crosscurrents, setting up blockages, and impeding main energies by dividing them in several diverse directions.
quotes.translation: 这些方向并非由任何法则强加于行动之上，它们只是能量表达自身时产生的模式。通常而言，你只以相当肤浅的方式熟悉这种动力或这些方向，因为自我宁愿不去感知它们。这些运动不过是行动所采取的流动。你所谓的负面暗示通常是阻碍行动，或阻碍主要内在方向性流动的行动方向。它们运作的方式类似于逆流，造成阻塞，并通过将主要能量分散到几个不同方向来阻碍它们。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

source_raw.title: （In Volume I of The Early Sessions this sensation, as I call it, is dealt with following Session 23, in Session 24, and following Session 25, among others. I have experienced it frequently during psychological time since then. It is much richer and more varied than the more usual thrilling one notes upon a moving occasion. In the above instance, the initial source of the sensation appeared to stem from outside the body, and the waves against my ears had a tangible, solid quality that is difficult to describe.

quotes.text: It isn't what you see, and yet what you see is it. Sight itself is it, the thing seen and the perceiver. You cannot stop action even in your mind, for the attempt to stop action is in itself an action. What you term death is an action. It is not the end of acts. The self at death is indeed more active as a rule than before, and the resources of the self are used to greater advantage.
quotes.translation: 它不是你所见之物，然而你所见之物即是它。视觉本身即是它，所见之物与感知者也是它。你甚至无法在思想中停止行动，因为停止行动的尝试本身就是一种行动。你所谓的死亡是一种行动，并非行动的终结。死亡时的自我通常比生前更为活跃，且自我的资源被更有效地运用。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

source_raw.title: (We received a letter from Pat Norelli today in which she substantiates some clairvoyant material concerning her, given by Seth in Session 340. Notes are included in that session.)

quotes.text: Such alternative reactions frighten the ego because they seem to injure the ego's self-image. Yet all characteristic reactions, whether denied by the ego or not, are kept for use as alternative actions. In many cases actions unacceptable to the ego may be precisely those actions that are necessary for whole other areas of the personality. When too many actions are restricted by the ego, they may begin to form impulse patterns or groupings of various rejected impulses. These then adhere through attraction, and attempt to find expression regardless of the ego's attempts to restrict expression.
quotes.translation: 这类替代性反应令自我恐惧，因为它们似乎损害了自我的自我形象。然而，所有特征性反应，无论是否被自我否认，都被保留作为替代行动。在许多情况下，自我无法接受的行动可能恰恰是人格其他完整领域所必需的行动。当太多行动被自我限制时，它们可能开始形成冲动模式或各种被拒绝冲动的集合。这些冲动随后通过吸引力相互依附，并试图不顾自我的限制而寻求表达。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The deeply and strongly dimensioned sphere I used as an analogy for an action, if you recall, for any portion of action; you can now indeed further imagine one entity being composed of such an action, with egos like many faces looking outward in all directions, and each perceiving vastly different fields of reality; looking inward and outward, backward and forward as it were, through and beyond. And yet each action, or entity, is a part of another, and is both within and without another. And none of it is meaningless, and yet in a basic manner all of it has the meaning that you give it.
quotes.translation: 我曾用深邃而强烈的维度球体来比喻一个行动——如果你还记得的话——比喻任何行动的任何部分；现在你确实可以进一步想象，一个存有由这样的行动构成，拥有许多面向外张望的自我，如同无数面孔，各自感知截然不同的实相领域；向内向外、前后上下地张望，穿透并超越一切。然而每个行动或存有都是另一个的一部分，既在另一个之内，又在另一个之外。这一切绝非无意义，但从根本而言，所有意义皆由你赋予。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: These acts are not only interpreted differently, they also of course have effects which are caused not only by the initial action but also by the distortive appearance of the action in a given system. The reaction to such a mental act is made not only to the act itself; but the reaction is made to the appearance of an initial act, as it is projected into the second system. Its very appearance or projection into a second system, therefore, causes an apparent change in the act itself. To all effects and purposes such an action becomes a different one, while in actuality it is the same.
quotes.translation: 这些行为不仅被不同地诠释，它们当然也产生效应——这些效应不仅由初始行动引发，还由该行动在特定系统中的扭曲表象所造成。对这种心智行为的反应不仅针对行为本身，更是针对初始行为投射入第二系统时的表象。因此，它进入第二系统的表象或投射本身，就使该行为发生了明显变化。从所有意图和目的来看，这样的行动变成了另一个行动，而实际上它仍是同一个。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Quite simply, the self travels to levels of awareness, and to layers of the self that are far divorced from the physical areas of mobility. The muscles are lax because activity of a physical nature is not required for the physical organism. Actions are indeed being carried on, and actions which would be considered physical if the body was moving, and if the individual were awake. These actions, walking, talking, working, any conceivable dream action, these require energy. The energy that is not being expended within the physical system is used to sustain these mental actions.
quotes.translation: 简单来说，自我会旅行至觉知的层次，以及远离肉体活动区域的自我层面。肌肉松弛，因为物理有机体不需要进行物质性质的活动。行动确实在进行着——那些若身体移动、若个体清醒时会被视为物理性的行动。这些行动——行走、交谈、工作，任何可想象的梦境行动——都需要能量。未在物理系统中消耗的能量，被用来维持这些心智行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: It is when significant actions, important to the whole personality, are so rejected that the difficulties arise. It is also true however that these refusals to assimilate action on the egos part are also an integral part of the characteristics of the personality as a whole action. In each individual, certain categories of action may be habitually denied. As the characteristics of a personality may be somewhat deduced from those actions which the ego accepts, so also much may be learned about any given personality by a study of those actions which the ego habitually denies.
quotes.translation: 当那些关乎整个人格的重要行动遭到如此排斥时，困难便随之产生。然而同样真实的是，这些拒绝在自我层面同化行动的行为，本身也是人格作为整体行动的特征中不可分割的一部分。每个个体都可能习惯性地否认某些类别的行动。正如人格特征可从自我所接纳的行动中略窥一二，通过研究自我习惯性否认的那些行动，同样能深入了解任何特定人格。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The multiplicity of human experience would be impossible were it not for the inner self, and for the heritage that speaks through the cells of every human being. The perspectives and psychic relationships that make up the human personality simply could not have resulted through action within your field alone. It cannot be stressed too strongly that experience within the self can lead to at least some understanding of the nature of action in its pure form, for within your physical universe action is to some extent frozen, in so far as your perceptions of it are concerned.
quotes.translation: 若非内在自我以及通过每个人细胞诉说的传承，人类经验的多样性将不可能存在。构成人格的视角与心灵关系，绝不可能仅凭你们领域内的行动而产生。必须再三强调：自我内在的经验至少能引导你理解纯粹形式的行动本质，因为在你们的物质宇宙中，就你们对它的感知而言，行动在某种程度上是被冻结的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Again, it may not involve motion in terms with which you usually refer. There are many kinds of motion, for example, that you do not perceive as motion within the physical field. Action tampers with identity, yet were it not for action identity would be impossible. It may, here, sound like a contradiction; but to remain an identity, an identity must completely renew itself, and each renewal is indeed a termination. Yet without the termination no new action on the part of the identity would be possible. And without action no identity can be aware of its own existence.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，它可能不涉及你们通常所指的运动。例如，有许多种运动在物质领域内你们并未感知为运动。行动会篡改身份，然而若非行动，身份便不可能存在。这听起来或许矛盾：但为了维持身份，身份必须彻底更新自身，而每次更新确实是一次终结。然而没有终结，身份就无法产生新的行动。没有行动，任何身份都无法意识到自身的存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: It acts upon itself, being action. The inner self also changes, but it is also that which changes itself. We come now close to a definition finally for the entity, which cannot really be defined, because in your terms it escapes definition. However, the entity can be partially defined as the sum of all the selves within a given range of action, the simultaneous totality which on the one hand then cannot yet exist, since action can never complete itself, yet representing that impetus forever frustrated on the part of action for complete materialization.
quotes.translation: 它作用于自身，因为它是行动。内在自己也改变，但它也是改变自身者。我们现在终于接近对存有的定义，这定义实际上无法被界定，因为用你们的说法，它超越了定义。然而，存有可被部分定义为特定行动范围内所有自己的总和，是同时性的整体——一方面它尚不存在，因为行动永远无法完成自身，却又代表着行动在完全物质化方面永远受挫的驱动力。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Because there is no time, as you think of time, we will not say that action retains a memory of all its previous actions or selves, for this would be misleading. Action is aware of itself in all of its spontaneous and simultaneous workings. The self that you are, in a basic sense, is the self that you were in past instances within this existence, the self or series of selves that you were in previous existences within the physical field, and also the myriad selves that you are now, in various perception experiences unknown to the ego.
quotes.translation: 由于没有你们所认为的时间，我们不会说行动保留着所有先前行动或自己的记忆，因为这会误导。行动在其所有自发且同时的运作中觉察自身。从根本意义上说，你所是的自己，就是你在这一存在中过去时刻所是的自己，你在物质领域内先前存在中所是的自己或一系列自己，以及你现在所是的无数自己——这些自己存在于自我未知的各种感知经验中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You perceive portions of such actions consciously, some subconsciously, some with the inner senses, but you do not perceive the whole action. Your perceptions, your physical perceptions, cut a good bit of the portion that you could receive into bits and pieces. Since you do not perceive whole action that occurs within your own system, you do not understand it. So it should not surprise you that you misinterpret projections of other actions from different systems, when they appear as they sometimes do within your own.
quotes.translation: 你有意识地感知到这类行动的部分，有些是潜意识地，有些通过内在感官，但你并未感知到完整的行动。你的感知，你的物质感知，将你本可接收的相当一部分切割成碎片。既然你无法感知自己系统内发生的完整行动，你便无法理解它。因此，当你误解来自不同系统的行动投射——当它们如有时那样出现在你的系统中时——这不应令你惊讶。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: I spoke, for example, of the acquiescence of action at certain levels to any kind of stimuli, indiscriminately, whether painful or pleasurable. Without this basic acquiescence, actions would not have been given the freedom to break patterns down and evolve new ones of them. This is not necessarily a more primitive aspect. It is simply a basic characteristic of action at certain levels, and the human personality, with its complicated ego structure, is nevertheless composed of many actions that operate at this level.
quotes.translation: 例如我曾说过，行动在某些层面上对任何刺激——无论痛苦或愉悦——都毫无分别地默许。若无此基本默许，行动便无法获得打破既有模式并演化新模式的自由。这未必是较原始的面向，而只是行动在特定层面的基本特性。人类人格虽具有复杂的自我结构，却仍由许多在此层面运作的行动所组成。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: They are brought into existence in a subjective manner, but they then are independent action, and as such may continue to exist in duration within the physical field, according to their original electric potential. In turn these thoughts or emotions, as electric actions, can affect other actions; and influence patterns can be set up, and are set up. The subjective habits of individuals are largely responsible for their own attractions to various types of such electric actions, and here indeed like attracts like.
quotes.translation: 这些行动以主观方式被带入存在，但随后成为独立行动，并依其原始电位在物理场域中持续存续。这些思想或情感作为电性行动，能影响其他行动，并可建立（且确实建立了）影响模式。个体主观习性很大程度上决定了他们会被何种电性行动吸引——在此确实遵循同类相吸法则。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Energy or action is composed of an infinity of itself, and yet, forever acting upon itself, it forms forever new portions of itself. Each action causes another. Now until we have done more talking you will simply have to take my word, for the sake of our discussion--our one-way discussion--that any action or energy possesses consciousness seeking to know itself, therefore; and acting within itself it forms new consciousness that are individual and independent, and yet connected to every other consciousness.
quotes.translation: 能量或行动由自身的无限性构成，却永远作用于自身，不断形成自身的新部分。每个行动都会引发另一个行动。在我们进行更多讨论之前——鉴于这场单向对话——你只能暂且相信我的说法：任何行动或能量都拥有寻求自我认知的意识，因此在其内部运作中，会形成既独立自主又与所有其他意识相连的新意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Now. I have just told you that one thought must terminate before another thought can appear. Although this might sound as if I am speaking in terms of continuity, I am not. The action of our imaginary sphere upon and within itself is simultaneous, and in all directions. All actions occur basically within the spacious present, but all action cannot be aware of itself except as it attempts further action, i.e., materializations.
quotes.translation: 现在。我刚刚告诉过你，一个念头必须终止，另一个念头才能显现。虽然这听起来似乎我在谈论连续性，但并非如此。我们假想的球体在其自身内部及对自身的行动是同时发生的，且向所有方向展开。所有行动基本上都发生在广袤的当下之内，但所有行动唯有在试图进一步行动（即物质化）时，才能觉察到自身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: As I have told you, all action is basically spontaneous. Only your perception of it adds the illusion of time to action. You think, for example, that any given action consumes or devours or takes up a certain amount of time. Therefore you think of time as something that contains action.
quotes.translation: 正如我告诉过你的，所有行动基本上都是自发的。只有你对它的感知才为行动添加了时间的幻相。例如，你认为任何既定的行动会消耗、吞噬或占用一定量的时间。因此，你把时间视为容纳行动的东西。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Each person, for example, is born with his or her uniquely individual set of characteristics and abilities, likes and dislikes. Those serve to organize individual action in a world where an infinite number of probable roads are open--and here again, private impulses are basically meant to guide each individual toward avenues of expression and probable activities suited best to his or her development. They are meant, therefore, as aids to help organize action (pause), and to set free will more effectively into motion. Otherwise, free will would be almost inoperable in practical terms: Individuals would be faced by so many choices that any decisions would be nearly impossible. Essentially, the individual would have no particular leaning toward any one action over any other (all with emphasis).
quotes.translation: 例如，每个人生来就带着自己独特的一套性格特质、能力、喜好与厌恶。这些特质在一个有无限可能道路敞开的世界上，用来组织个体的行动——在此再次强调，私密的冲动基本上旨在引导每个个体走向最适合其发展的表达途径与可能活动。因此，它们被设计为协助组织行动的辅助工具（停顿），并让自由意志更有效地运作起来。否则，自由意志在实际上几乎无法运作：个体将面对如此众多的选择，以至于任何决定都几乎不可能。本质上，个体对任何特定行动都不会有特别的倾向性（全部强调）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I am speaking now of the basic nature of reality, and not of any particular action, for particular actions can indeed be altered by other actions, and no given action occurs in a solitary manner. This last is extremely important. Actions are perceived as realities according to the nature, not of a given action, but according to the nature of the perceiver. His viewpoint and his field of reference will at all times color to some extent or another the nature of the reality which he perceives.
quotes.translation: 我现在谈论的是实相的基本本质，而非任何特定行动，因为特定行动确实可以被其他行动改变，且没有任何行动是孤立发生的。最后这一点极为重要。行动被视为实相的依据，并非取决于特定行动的本质，而是取决于感知者的本质。他的视角与参照系，总会以某种程度影响他所感知的实相本质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: A note now concerning thought, as it is a form of action with which all men are familiar. Here you can see that your ego accepts thoughts as a part of its identity. Thought's actions are accepted by the ego, yet the ego seems to stand apart from them; and because of ego's nature it fears to plunge into the action of a thought. For it, the ego, has but recently pried itself from action, and so perceives action now as if action were a province of the ego, and not the other way around.
quotes.translation: 现在关于思想的一点说明——思想是人类熟悉的一种行动形式。在此你可以看到，自我将思想接纳为自身身份的一部分。自我接受思想的活动，却又似乎与之保持距离；由于自我的本质，它畏惧投入思想的行动中。因为自我刚刚才从行动中挣脱出来，如今将行动视为自我的领地，而非相反。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Here the self, by becoming part of greater action, increases its own ability to deal with action. The principle that action acts upon itself is extremely important when we are dealing with psychological action. The principle that action is self-generating, and that it cannot be withdrawn, is also vital in connection with psychological action.
quotes.translation: 在此，自体通过成为更大行动的一部分，提升了自身处理行动的能力。当我们处理心理行动时，"行动作用于自身"这一原则极为重要。"行动是自我生成的，且无法撤回"这一原则，与心理行动同样密切相关。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The psychological feeling of intensity has its own electromagnetic reality. An action is an experienced intensity, and need not involve motion in physical terms. As I have said, every action is a part of every other action, and affects every other action, and is also so affected itself. This is not however your cause and effect theory at all.
quotes.translation: 心理上的强烈感受有其自身的电磁实相。行动是一种被体验到的强度，并不必然涉及物质层面的运动。如我所说，每个行动都是其他每个行动的一部分，影响其他每个行动，同时也被其影响。然而这完全不是你们所谓的因果理论。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: The action involved in these sessions, for example, changes us all, yet truly none of us perceive the nature of the entire action of which we are a part. I, for example, cannot perceive the entire future consequences of any one action. I may perceive the entire consequences of any given action within your system or my own, but it is impossible for me to perceive a given action's consequences as it is felt within all systems, for each action occurs within all systems simultaneously.
quotes.translation: 举例来说，这些课中所涉及的行动改变了我们所有人，但事实上我们中没有人能感知到我们所属的整个行动的本质。例如，我无法感知任何一个行动的完整未来后果。我或许能感知到某个特定行动在你们系统或我自己的系统内的全部后果，但我无法感知该行动在所有系统中的感受，因为每个行动同时发生于所有系统之中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Lest we forget, action is another word for inner vitality. Selves are formed also by effective ranges of comprehension, which may be expanded. They cannot be contracted, for action cannot wipe out comprehension of itself. The electrically coded data of which we have spoken cannot be removed, for an action cannot withdraw a previous action.
quotes.translation: 别忘了，行动是内在活力的另一个说法。自我也由可扩展的理解有效范围所形成。它们无法被收缩，因为行动无法抹除对自身的理解。我们曾提及的电性编码数据无法被移除，因为一个行动无法撤回先前的行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: There is no cause and effect in the terms in which you understand the words. Nor is there a succession of moments that follow one after the other; and without a succession of moments following one after the other you can see that the idea of cause and effect becomes meaningless. An action of the present in your terms cannot be based or caused by an action in the past, and neither action can be the cause of a future action in a basic reality where neither past nor future exist.
quotes.translation: 你们所理解的因果律并不存在。也没有前后相继的瞬间序列；既然没有前后相继的瞬间序列，你们就能看出因果观念变得毫无意义。在你们认知的当下，一个行动不可能基于或由过去的行动引发，而在既无过去也无未来的基本实相中，任何行动都不能成为未来行动的因。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Action can never be considered apart from that which is seemingly acted upon, for action becomes a part of structure. Action begins from within, and is a result of inner vitality inherent within all realities. Some action is always present. Action itself is not a thing alone. It is not an identity. Action is a dimension of existence.
quotes.translation: 行动绝不能与看似被作用的对象分开来考虑，因为行动会成为结构的一部分。行动从内在开始，是一切实相内在活力的结果。行动始终存在。行动本身并非孤立之物，也不是某种身份认同。行动是存在的一个维度。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: What you call suggestion then is but a small aspect of a larger directive characteristic that is ever part of action itself. It is indeed in the nature of an impetus, an inner impetus that belongs to action, and is not some force separated from action, and acting upon it. This impetus is a natural and spontaneous movement that springs from within action itself. It can even be termed the direction, or the various spontaneous directions, in which action itself moves.
quotes.translation: 你们所谓的暗示，不过是行动本身始终具有的更大导向性特征的一个微小面向。它本质上是一种冲力，一种属于行动的内在冲力，而非某种脱离行动并作用于行动的外力。这种冲力是源于行动本身的自发运动。它甚至可以被称作行动自身运动的方向，或各种自发性的方向。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

source_raw.title: （In Volume I of The Early Sessions this sensation, as I call it, is dealt with following Session 23, in Session 24, and following Session 25, among others. I have experienced it frequently during psychological time since then. It is much richer and more varied than the more usual thrilling one notes upon a moving occasion. In the above instance, the initial source of the sensation appeared to stem from outside the body, and the waves against my ears had a tangible, solid quality that is difficult to describe.

quotes.text: Any gap of assimilation here can be most unfortunate, and sometimes disastrous. Consciousness of self, if you recall, is self-consciousness that still retains self as a part of action, self that perceives its existence within action. Ego, originally a part of this consciousness of self, splits off as previously explained, and attempts to dissociate itself from action, indeed to view action as a result of itself; that is, to view action as a result and not a cause.
quotes.translation: 此处任何同化的间隙都可能极为不幸，有时甚至带来灾难。自我意识，若你记得，是仍将自我视为行动一部分的自觉意识，是感知自身存在于行动之中的自我。原本作为这种自我意识一部分的自我，如先前所述发生分裂，试图与行动分离，甚至将行动视为自身的结果；也就是说，将行动视为结果而非原因。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The inner self would be then any given outthrust of original action outward, as explained earlier. This outthrust would, because of its nature, instantly send further outthrusts in as many directions as possible for it. And because it is action, and because no action can complete itself, and no action can completely materialize, then each outthrust or materialization would result in an in-thrust; not into the original action from which it came, but into itself.
quotes.translation: 内在自体便是任何原始行动向外投射的特定展现，如前所述。这种投射因其本质，会立即向尽可能多的方向发出进一步的投射。由于它是行动，且没有任何行动能自我完成，也没有任何行动能完全物质化，因此每一次投射或物质化都会导致一次内收；并非回到它所源自的原始行动，而是进入自身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The inner self here, through intuitive insight, can usually recognize whether an action is an impeding or a constructive one for the purposes of the personality involved. Even an action which appears blatantly as an impeding action, may temporarily serve as a constructive one. It may then turn into an impeding action.
quotes.translation: 在此，内在自体通过直觉洞察，通常能辨识某个行动对涉及的人格目的是阻碍性的还是建设性的。即使一个明显表现为阻碍性的行动，也可能暂时发挥建设性作用，随后再转变为阻碍性行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

source_raw.title: （In Volume I of The Early Sessions this sensation, as I call it, is dealt with following Session 23, in Session 24, and following Session 25, among others. I have experienced it frequently during psychological time since then. It is much richer and more varied than the more usual thrilling one notes upon a moving occasion. In the above instance, the initial source of the sensation appeared to stem from outside the body, and the waves against my ears had a tangible, solid quality that is difficult to describe.

quotes.text: We have in the past discussed in full the manner in which physical matter is formed. We have also discussed the ego, and our friend would do well to read the sessions dealing with the ego, and the ego's relationship to action. For one of the basic reasons for the difficulty lies in the fact that the ego of the present personality does strongly attempt to stand apart from action. It attempts to force action, and to fight action which it does not initiate.
quotes.translation: 我们过去曾详细讨论过物质形态的形成方式，也讨论过自我。我们的朋友最好能重读那些探讨自我及其与行动关系的章节。因为造成困境的根本原因之一，在于当前人格的自我确实强烈试图与行动保持距离。它试图强迫行动，并抗拒并非由它发起的行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Actually, you consider action which seems to be at least partially self-directive as living matter. Action that seems to be of a static nature, you refer to as inert matter. It should go without saying that all action is indeed self-directed action to some degree, and therefore should be termed as living matter.
quotes.translation: 实际上，你们将那些至少看似具有部分自我导向性的行动视为有生命的物质，而将看似静态的行动称为惰性物质。不言而喻的是，所有行动在某种程度上确实都是自我导向的行动，因此都应被称为有生命的物质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: It is this dilemma, precisely between identity's constant attempts to maintain stability, and actions inherent drive for change, that results in the imbalance, the exquisite creative by-product that is consciousness of self. We have a series of creative strains. Identity must seek stability while action must seek change, yet identity could not exist without change, without action, for it is the result of action, and not apart from it but a part of it.
quotes.translation: 正是这种两难困境——身份认同不断试图维持稳定，与行动固有的变革驱力之间的冲突——导致了失衡，也产生了那精妙的创造性副产品：自我意识。我们有一系列创造性的张力。身份认同必须寻求稳定，而行动必须寻求改变，然而没有改变、没有行动，身份认同就无法存在——因为它是行动的结果，并非独立于行动之外，而是行动的一部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: First I want to make certain that action is understood, insofar as we have discussed it. Action of the inner vitality of all reality. It seeks to utilize itself in as many ways as possible. Its action, its attempts at outward materialization, however, must result in the creation of new inner vitality, for this is the stuff of which it is composed. And this new inner vitality will then seek materialization, and so the cycle is never completed.
quotes.translation: 首先我要确保你们理解"行动"这个概念——就我们讨论过的范围而言。行动是一切实相的内在生命力。它试图以尽可能多的方式运用自身。然而，它的行动、它向外物质化的尝试，必然导致新内在生命力的创造，因为这就是构成它的本质。而这新的内在生命力随后会寻求物质化，如此循环永无止境。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: It must be understood that the personality is indeed an action within action, and that it is therefore never stationary. Indeed suggestions, being the directions in which action moves, represent the very impetus that constantly changes the action of any given personality. It goes without saying, once more, that all of these designations imply a separation which does not exist in fact, and imply definite boundaries which are not present.
quotes.translation: 必须明白，人格确实是行动中的行动，因此它永不停滞。事实上，暗示作为行动移动的方向，代表着持续改变任何特定人格行动的原动力。毋庸赘言，所有这些指称都暗示着现实中并不存在的分离，也暗示着并不存在的明确界限。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

source_raw.title: （In Volume I of The Early Sessions this sensation, as I call it, is dealt with following Session 23, in Session 24, and following Session 25, among others. I have experienced it frequently during psychological time since then. It is much richer and more varied than the more usual thrilling one notes upon a moving occasion. In the above instance, the initial source of the sensation appeared to stem from outside the body, and the waves against my ears had a tangible, solid quality that is difficult to describe.

quotes.text: Such a rejection is definitely an impeding action. It is this rejection on the ego's part that is the basis for so-called neurosis in many cases. The fault is not that a particular action has been buried by the subconscious. The fault is that the ego has refused to accept the action from the subconscious, therefore impeding the natural flow of energy. Naturally, all actions are not recognized by the ego, nor is it necessary in any case.
quotes.translation: 这种拒绝显然是一种阻碍性行动。在许多案例中，正是自我层面的这种拒绝构成了所谓神经症的基础。问题不在于某个特定行动被潜意识埋藏，而在于自我拒绝接受来自潜意识的行动，从而阻碍了能量的自然流动。当然，并非所有行动都会被自我认知，而且任何情况下都无需如此。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: One thing you should know: action cannot stop. It is powerless in this respect only. It cannot destroy itself. It can change form. It often changes direction. It is so many-dimensional that it must experience itself as individuals. Individuality is a direct result of the overall oneness of this action. Action is also a direct result of individual identities, for without these psychological dimensions, oneness could not multiply itself.
quotes.translation: 你该知道一件事：行动无法停止。它仅在此方面无能为力。它无法毁灭自身。它可以改变形式。它时常改变方向。它如此多维，以至于必须以个体身份体验自身。个体性是这整体行动的直接结果。行动也是个体身份的直接结果，因为没有这些心理维度，整体性便无法繁衍自身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: You both know again what love and hate are, but as I told you before try to think in new ways. Love and hate for example are action. They are both action and they both imply action in physical bodies, and even as far as thoughts are concerned. In your plane action is the main word of importance.
quotes.translation: 你们再次明白了爱与恨的本质，但正如我先前所言，请尝试以全新方式思考。例如爱与恨皆是行动。它们既是行动，也暗示着物质身体中的行动，甚至延伸至思想层面。在你们的层面中，行动是至关重要的关键词。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: I enjoy the emotional rapport among us here... our discussions are indeed action. Action changes me, and everyone in this room. I am no exception, even though I am here in a less conventional form. Actions change us all. We must change. I enjoy the actions... I also enjoy the buildup of your ideas. I am sincerely interested in all of your ideas--so that I can correct them. But this does not mean we cannot have an informal atmosphere.
quotes.translation: 我享受我们之间的情感共鸣……我们的讨论确实是行动。行动改变着我，也改变着这房间里的每个人。我亦不例外，即便我以非传统形态存在于此。行动改变我们所有人。我们必须改变。我享受这些行动……也享受你们思想的累积。我真诚关注你们所有的想法——以便修正它们。但这不意味着我们不能保持轻松氛围。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: In other words the apparent intensity of the egotistical reactions is a sham on the part of the ego, to hide the fact that it refuses to become involved with action as a whole, because it fears for its permanence. When the ego understands, and it will, that it is a portion of the whole self, and itself a part of action, then indeed it will not fear for its own permanence, for it will realize that being a part of action, its very nature is dependent upon change, and vitality, and value fulfillment.
quotes.translation: 换言之，自我式反应的表面强度其实是自我的伪装，用以掩盖它拒绝参与整体行动的事实，因为它害怕自身存续的永久性。当自我明白——它终将明白——自己是整体自性的一部分，也是行动的一部分时，它便不再恐惧自身的存续，因为它会认识到，作为行动的一部分，其本质依赖于变化、活力与价值完成。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: When the lines to the original action are released and the channels opened to it, the illness will vanish. In some cases however, you see, the thwarted action may be one with disastrous consequences, which the illness may prevent. The personality has a logic of its own. We will be involved for many sessions to come with these problems, for they are of basic value, and practical value. We will, then, later discuss the manner in which the difference can be seen between a beneficial illness, and a severely detrimental one. We will see how a temporarily necessary illness can be greatly lessened, and the symptoms minimized, while the illness is still retained as a temporary emergency measure, and then gradually allowed to disappear when its presence becomes unnecessary.
quotes.translation: 当与原始行动的联结被释放，通往它的通道被打开时，疾病便会消失。然而在某些情况下，受挫的行动可能带有灾难性后果，而疾病恰恰能阻止这种后果。人格有其自身的逻辑。我们将在未来许多节课中探讨这些问题，因为它们具有基础价值与实践价值。随后我们将讨论如何区分有益疾病与严重有害疾病，以及如何大幅减轻暂时必要疾病的症状，在保留其作为临时应急措施的同时，逐步在其不再必要时令其消失。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: He did voluntarily choose to be born as a son in this existence. Now he rationalizes on a conscious level his reasons for remaining home. You said earlier that around the school there was the expression he "loves them and leaves them". You see, a very cruel interpretation and a very literal interpretation of his action in a past life, this coming through in an entirely different situation in this life. He is, of course, aware subconsciously of this and acts in such a way for he feels more honest. Through his actions in this life he is trying to make an honest statement about actions in the past. There is, of course, no punishment involved. His secrecy also is a direct result of these past existences, for once he spoke too much and betrayed too much, so now he remains secretive about matters that he considers important. The two brothers never did hold him responsible, however. They understood the situation. They knew that the girl had been terrified and spoke only out of fear and did not mean to betray them. In this life, then, the parents do not mean to hold him. They are not subconsciously trying to chain him; they are not subconsciously trying to bind him. He has chosen to act in this particular manner. He would be much freer if he'd realize that the brothers do not hold him responsible. And the betrayal, while a betrayal, was understandable, and that he spoke out of fear and did not intend to betray. If you will forgive me, I do not think we should use time in the session with your question about your friends poetry. It is not important in comparison to other information, nor is it important to the man's development, nor to your own. Now I know that you speak of me highly; and if I wore a hat, I would tip it to you. You are helping other people and you will continue to do so. Now that you have some insight into the reasons for certain difficulties, you can begin to do something about them. Your problem is not with your Mr. Reed. Your problem is to rid yourself of the image that you have that you project upon him.
quotes.translation: 他确实自愿选择在这一世投生为儿子。如今他在意识层面合理化自己留在家中的理由。你之前提到，在学校周围流传着关于他"爱过就离开"的说法。你看，这是对他前世行为极为残酷且字面化的解读，却在这一世以完全不同的情境呈现出来。他当然在潜意识中知晓这一点，并以此方式行事，因为他觉得这样更诚实。通过这一世的行为，他试图对过去的行为做出诚实的陈述。这当然不涉及惩罚。他的隐秘性也是这些前世经历的直接结果——因为曾经言多必失、泄露过多，所以如今他对自认为重要的事情守口如瓶。然而那两位兄弟从未责怪过他。他们理解当时的情况，知道那女孩因恐惧而泄密，并非有意背叛。在这一世，父母也无意束缚他。他们并非在潜意识中试图捆绑他。是他自己选择了以这种方式行事。如果他意识到兄弟们并未责怪他，他会自由得多。那次背叛虽是背叛，却是情有可原的——他出于恐惧而泄密，并非存心背叛。请恕我直言，我认为我们不应在课中花时间回答你关于朋友诗歌的问题。与其他信息相比，这既不重要，对那位朋友的发展或你自己的成长也无足轻重。我知道你对我赞誉有加；若我戴着帽子，定会向你脱帽致意。你正在帮助他人，并将继续如此。既然你已对某些困境的成因有所洞见，便可着手应对。你的问题不在于里德先生，而在于摆脱你投射在他身上的那个形象。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

source_raw.title: (I thought it a good idea that this be the end of the session, however, Seth came through again at 10:30 as the three of us sat talking about various things. A humorous repartee followed, with Seth answering our questions on a variety of subjects. Two serious notes: Alice Butts, who died last week, has already visited my father. And Dr. Instream, who would say "There is no Seth, " has not much longer to live. Also -- Bill Gallagher will not see certain truths about himself etc. Alice Butts not helping Ruth Butts ' son, etc. See Session 398.)

quotes.text: This in no manner affects the nature of those actions which you ignore. However, to all intents and purposes it changes action in so far as you are concerned with it. This would therefore imply a choice on your part, in a manner that you do not now enjoy. For you can then choose to perceive advantageous action, as well as you can choose not to perceive action which for any reason or another you choose to ignore.
quotes.translation: 这丝毫不影响那些你忽略的行动的本质。然而，就你与它的关联而言，它在所有实际意义上改变了行动。因此，这暗示了你拥有一种选择权，其方式是你目前尚未享有的。因为你可以选择感知有利的行动，也可以选择不去感知那些因任何理由而被你忽略的行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The reality of such an identity then exists within the action. The energy of the action, the workings of action within and upon itself, forms identity. There are other causes here that we will consider later. Yet although identity is formed from action, action and identity cannot be separated. You will remember our previous definition of action, for this will make this evening's discussion easier to understand.
quotes.translation: 这种身份的现实性于是存在于行动之内。行动的能量、行动在其自身内部及作用于自身的运作，形成了身份。这里还有其他原因，我们稍后会讨论。然而，尽管身份由行动形成，行动与身份却不可分割。你会记得我们之前对行动的定义，因为这会让今晚的讨论更容易理解。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: This is not the case, for your own expectations are the actions which mold what you call the future, and it is never static and never definite; for you can change it at any moment, as any action changes any other action. You are always free to act, but every action changes that which is acted upon, and you constantly change your so-called future; and the events that I see may indeed be changed at any time.
quotes.translation: 情况并非如此，因为你自己的期待正是塑造你所谓未来的行动，而未来从不静止，也从不确定；因为你可以在任何时刻改变它，正如任何行动改变其他任何行动。你始终有行动的自由，但每个行动都会改变它所作用的对象，而你不断改变你所谓的未来；我所看见的事件确实可以在任何时候被改变。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: The ego attempts to break down action into smaller and smaller units. The intuitions try to perceive action as a whole. The ego breaks down for purposes of examination, the intuitions construct. Both the ego and the intuitions, in performing their functions, obviously create action. The self may of course be considered as a gestalt of action, perceived in a different manner by various levels of itself.
quotes.translation: 自我试图将行动分解为越来越小的单位。直觉则试图将行动视为一个整体。自我为了检视的目的而分解，直觉则进行建构。自我与直觉在执行各自功能时，显然都在创造行动。自体当然可被视为行动的一个完形，由其自身的不同层面以不同方式所感知。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Action which is materialized within your plane appears within your field, and disappears from your field. This in no way affects the basic nature of action itself. That is, it does not change the basic laws of action. Action will change both within your physical field and outside your physical field. You are only aware of a small part of action, that portion which is materialized within your system.
quotes.translation: 在你们层面物质化的行动，出现在你们的场域中，并从你们的场域中消失。这丝毫不会影响行动本身的基本性质。也就是说，它不会改变行动的基本法则。行动既会在你们的物质场域内改变，也会在你们的物质场域外改变。你们只意识到行动的一小部分，即那些在你们系统内物质化的部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: We will continue our discussion concerning action and identities. I have said that identity is a part of action, and basically inseparable from action. Identity attempts to form meaningful patterns and relationships from action. Consciousness is action that perceives itself. The ego is action's attempt to stand off from itself.
quotes.translation: 我们将继续讨论关于行动与身份认同的话题。我曾说过，身份认同是行动的一部分，且基本上与行动不可分割。身份认同试图从行动中形成有意义的模式与关系。意识是感知自身的行动。自我则是行动试图与自身保持距离的尝试。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: It will refuse to assimilate certain experiences. This very refusal is in itself an action. It will attempt to construct itself on a physical level, since it is within the physical system of reality. The cooperation is broken down to some greater or lesser degree. This conflicts with the inner data in the case of illnesses, and the illnesses are actually the result of conflicts of data. It is obvious that the body is equipped to handle many such distorted actions, but a conflict is hereby brought into play, where constructive energies or actions are not given full reign, and patterns of distortions are set up within the system.
quotes.translation: 它将拒绝同化某些经验。这种拒绝本身就是一种行动。由于它处于物质实相系统内，它会试图在物质层面构建自身。合作会在不同程度上被破坏。在疾病的情况下，这与内在资料相冲突，而疾病实际上是资料冲突的结果。显然，身体具备处理许多此类扭曲行动的能力，但由此引发了一种冲突，其中建设性的能量或行动无法得到充分施展，系统内便形成了扭曲的模式。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The inner ego directs all activities which are deeply subjective. The outer ego as you know deals with the manipulation of the self in the physical environment. You must remember that the whole self is more than the sum of its parts; and also, since it is action, it is never the same. It is therefore impossible at any moment to pinpoint the personality. Therefore the whole self is not only the sum of the personality as you know it in your time, it is also the sum of what it has been and what it shall be. For as I have explained action is simultaneous, and time as you think of it is caused by your own physical perception.
quotes.translation: 内在自我指挥所有深度主观的活动。如你所知，外在自我处理自我在物质环境中的操控。你必须记住，整体自我大于其各部分的总和；而且，由于它是行动，它永远不会相同。因此，在任何时刻都不可能准确定位人格。所以，整体自我不仅是你时间中已知的人格总和，也是它过去和未来所是的一切的总和。因为正如我所解释的，行动是同时性的，而你所认为的时间是由你自身的物质感知造成的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: No action basically is ever completed. This statement will later lead us into further realms. All possibilities are open to an action, an electrical action. And again, within it are an infinity of variations and depths of intensities, which give it a reality in many dimensions simultaneously. Where strong intensities are felt, then the actuality will be said to project within the field of reality which falls within the range of that particular intensity group.
quotes.translation: 从根本上说，没有行动是完成的。这个陈述稍后将引导我们进入更深的领域。所有可能性都对一个行动、一个电性行动敞开。同样，在其内部存在着无限的变数和强度深度，使其同时在许多维度中具有实相。当感受到强烈的强度时，实际性将被说成投射在属于该特定强度群范围内的实相场域中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now. Action is something like a mirror which reflects itself. In one action, basically, we can see all actions, and through one action we can reach the reality of all actions.
quotes.translation: 现在。行动就像一面映照自身的镜子。在一个行动中，我们基本上能看见所有行动，而透过一个行动，我们能触及所有行动的真实性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Action itself cannot be directly perceived. It cannot be seen nor touched. Its nature can never be examined from an objective viewpoint. The objective viewpoint will, at best, give but hints and signs. Action, to be examined in such a manner, would have to be stopped. You cannot tamper with action, not with the basic nature of action, because any such tampering causes it to change.
quotes.translation: 行动本身无法被直接感知。它既不能被看见，也不能被触摸。它的本质永远无法从客观的角度来检视。客观的观点充其量只能提供线索和迹象。要以这种方式检视行动，就必须让行动停止。你无法干预行动，也无法干预行动的基本本质，因为任何这样的干预都会导致它改变。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Action accepts all stimuli in an affirmative manner. It is only when action becomes compartmented, so to speak, in the development of highly differentiated consciousness, that such refinement occurs. I am not here saying that unpleasant stimuli will not be felt as unpleasant, and reacted against, by less self-conscious organisms. I am saying that less self-conscious organisms will rejoice even in their automatic reaction against such stimuli, because any stimuli and reaction represents sensation, and sensation is another method by which such action knows and expresses itself.
quotes.translation: 行动以肯定的方式接纳所有刺激。只有当行动在高度分化意识的发展中被分割成区块时，才会产生这种精炼。我并不是说较不具自我意识的生物不会感受到不愉快的刺激，并对此做出反应。我是说，较不具自我意识的生物甚至会在对这种刺激的自动反应中感到喜悦，因为任何刺激和反应都代表着感受，而感受是这种行动认识并表达自身的另一种方式。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Your own experiments in psychological time have allowed you, to some extent, to experience within yourselves such unimpeded action. You know now that the ego, because of its nature, attempts to set itself aside from action. It obviously cannot do so. The ego, being part of action, nevertheless affects the nature of action as seen in the various manifestations of the whole self.
quotes.translation: 你们在心理时间中的实验，已在某种程度上让你们体验到自身内部这种不受阻碍的行动。你们现在知道，自我因其本质，试图将自己与行动分离。它显然无法做到。自我作为行动的一部分，却影响着行动的本质，这体现在整个自我的各种显化中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Your effective power of action follows the lines of your beliefs. To believe in your own weakness is to deny yourself the power of action. To accept uncritically all beliefs that come to you is to open yourself to a barrage of conflicting data at best, in which the clear lines of action and power become blurred. Contradictory demands and assessments are then sent in to the inner self, which by various methods will try to tell you that something is wrong. Beliefs of like nature attract each other, for you are bound to look for consistencies in your behavior and experience.
quotes.translation: 你们有效的行动力量遵循着信念的路线。相信自己的软弱，就是否定自己的行动力量。不加批判地接受所有来到你面前的信念，充其量只会让你暴露在相互冲突的信息轰炸中，使行动与力量的清晰路线变得模糊。矛盾的要求与评估随后被送入内在自我，而内在自我会通过各种方法试图告诉你出了差错。性质相似的信念会相互吸引，因为你必然会在自己的行为与经验中寻求一致性。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You cannot touch the action. You cannot touch the action, now, of your own arm as you write. You see the results of the action. You can feel effects of the action, but you cannot directly perceive the action itself. Since identity is dependent upon action, then it should be seen that it is impossible for an identity to attain stability, since total stability would destroy it.
quotes.translation: 你无法触摸行动。此刻，当你书写时，你无法触摸自己手臂的行动。你看到行动的结果。你能感受到行动的效果，但你无法直接感知行动本身。既然身份认同依赖于行动，那么应当明白，身份认同不可能获得稳定，因为完全的稳定会摧毁它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Many -- not all -- plotless novels or movies are the result of this belief in man's powerlessness. In that context no action is heroic, and man is everywhere the victim of an alien universe. On the other hand your common, unlettered, violent television dramas do indeed provide a service, for they imaginatively specify a generalized fear in a given situation, which is then resolved through drama. Individual action counts. The plots may be stereotyped or the acting horrendous, but in the most conventional terms the "good" man wins.
quotes.translation: 许多——并非全部——无情节的小说或电影，正是源于这种对人类无力感的信念。在此语境下，没有任何行动是英勇的，人类处处成为陌生宇宙的受害者。另一方面，你们那些通俗、粗鄙、暴力的电视剧确实提供了某种服务，因为它们以想象力将普遍化的恐惧具体呈现在特定情境中，并通过戏剧性冲突得到化解。个体的行动至关重要。情节或许刻板，演技或许拙劣，但以最传统的标准而言，"好人"终将获胜。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 805, MAY 16, 1977
9:28 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Any action that affects the physical individual also has its reality within many other fields, and its effects and its nature are felt within them. A manuscript, or indeed any art form, contains action and sets up its own climate, either of psychological acceptance or rejection. This climate is more than the result of the materials or subject matter or nature of which the work is composed. It goes without saying that such a work actually contains a portion of focused psychic energy, which is action, and which has its effects.
quotes.translation: 任何影响物质个体的行动，其真实性与本质也会在其他诸多场域中显现，并产生相应效应。一份手稿，或任何艺术形式，都蕴含着行动，并营造出自身特有的氛围——无论是心理上的接纳还是排斥。这种氛围远非作品的材料、主题或本质所能概括。毋庸置疑，这类作品实际上包含着聚焦的心灵能量，这能量本身就是行动，并持续产生着影响。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: We come here also to one of the other causes of the dreaming state, beside those of which we have spoken in the past. The mind, of itself and separated from the ego, must still be action, and therefore never still. Since action of any kind, being composed of inner vitality, must seek materialization, the dreams become the constructions of that dream universe of which, again, we have spoken. But action can never complete itself. The dream once begun continues, and the dream universe itself forms anew other constructions.
quotes.translation: 在此我们还需探讨梦境状态的另一成因，以补充先前讨论的内容。心灵本身，当脱离自我意识时，仍必须是行动，因此永不停歇。由于任何行动都由内在活力构成，必然寻求物质化呈现，于是梦境便成为我们曾提及的那个梦宇宙的建构。但行动永远无法自我完成。一旦开始的梦境会持续演进，而梦宇宙本身又会衍生出新的建构。
