source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Your conception of time is dependent upon your perception of action, and that portion of action which you can perceive and appreciate. There is no doubt here that the intuitions can at times perceive action far more completely than can the intellect. And time, therefore, physical clock time, is much more alien to the intuitive self than it is to the intellectual self.
quotes.translation: 你对时间的观念取决于你对行动的看法，以及你所能感知和欣赏的那部分行动。毫无疑问，直觉有时能比理智更完整地感知行动。因此，时间——尤其是物理时钟时间——对直觉性自我而言，远比理智性自我更为陌生。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: The designations put upon these units of action are highly artificial, and represent limitations that are quite arbitrary. We mentioned in an earlier session something to this effect, in that you simply perceive a small portion of such action, label it as a unit, can perceive no further, and so suppose that what you see or perceive is all there is to see or perceive.
quotes.translation: 加诸这些行动单位之上的名称是高度人为的，代表着相当武断的限制。我们在早些时候的课中曾提及类似观点：你仅仅感知到这类行动的一小部分，将其标记为一个单位，便无法再深入感知，于是便以为你所见或所感知的就是全部可见或可感知的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: In other words, the inclusion of my personality characteristics with the material will take away any long white gowned spiritualistic connotations, and put the survival personality thesis in a more proper light. As any good writer should know, tell Ruburt he need not explain my characteristics; simply include excerpts that show them. If he does this to the best of his ability, he will consider his excerpts from two standpoints--what to say, and how I say it, so that the personality and the material are clearly shown together. It will make it lively. (Long pause) The personality is action, and as I have shown myself to you in sessions, this has added to the action of the sessions.
quotes.translation: 换言之，将我的个性特征融入资料中，将消除任何长袍飘飘的灵媒式联想，使存续人格的论点以更恰当的方式呈现。正如任何优秀作家所应知的，告诉鲁柏他无需解释我的特征，只需摘录能展现这些特征的片段即可。若他尽其所能，便应从两个角度考量其摘录——该说什么，以及我如何说，使个性与资料清晰共现。这将使其生动鲜活。（长停顿）人格即行动，正如我在课中向你们展现的那样，这已为课程增添了行动力。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: So, while each action of your life is taken in context with all other actions of your life until your death, this does not mean that your death is predestined to occur at any given time. As you might change your sentence in the middle from one version to another without even being consciously aware of it, so as you live your life you also work with probabilities. You are the self who speaks the sentence, and you are the self who lives the life. You are larger than the sentence that you speak, and larger than the life you live.
quotes.translation: 因此，虽然你生命中的每个行动都与你直至死亡的所有其他行动相互关联，但这并不意味着你的死亡注定在某个特定时刻发生。正如你可能会在句子中间无意识地将其从一个版本改为另一个版本，你在生活的同时也在处理或然率。你是说出句子的那个自己，也是活出人生的那个自己。你比你说出的句子更宏大，也比你活出的人生更宏大。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: "All action is creative and ever-occurring--the only way I can get these ideas across to you. All energy is personified. When you look at atoms and stars you are looking at simultaneous action. You are looking into the past and into the future at the same time, as you think of it. From the landscape of the brain you are trying to look at the landscape of the mind.
quotes.translation: "所有行动都是创造性的且永恒发生的——这是我唯一能将理念传达给你的方式。所有能量都是人格化的。当你观察原子与星辰时，你正在目睹同步行动。以你的认知方式，你同时看到了过去与未来。你正试图从大脑的景观中眺望心灵的景观。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 2. Sometimes together, sometimes separately, Jane and I listen to portions of the tapes students had made of the sessions and other exciting happenings in her ESP classes. I'd hardly put into shape for Session 933 my musings about the workings of consciousness than I heard Seth come through with these two passages; at once they reminded me of what I'd just been writing about.

quotes.text: Vitamins, better food, medical attention, may temporarily rejuvenate the body, but unless you change your beliefs it will quickly become swamped again by your feelings of depression. In such a case you must realize that you make your own loneliness, and resolve to change through both thought and action. Action is thought in physical motion, outwardly perceived.
quotes.translation: 维生素、更优质的食物、医疗护理或许能暂时恢复身体活力，但除非你改变信念，否则它很快会再次被你的抑郁感受淹没。在这种情况下，你必须明白是你制造了自己的孤独，并决心通过思想与行动来改变。行动是思想在物质运动中的外在显化。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Ruburt sensed one in particular, and did indeed react in a manner that you found annoying. Ruburt's feelings and lack of action as far as the publishing house was concerned left much to be desired, and in time his lack of action would have caused an unpleasant reaction on his own part. However he correctly, if subconsciously, interpreted your attitude toward the publishing house as being basically dangerous to you. And so it was.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏确实感应到某个特定情况，并以你觉得恼人的方式做出了反应。就出版社而言，鲁柏的感受与不作为实在令人遗憾，而长期的不作为终将引发他自身的不良反应。不过，他正确（即便是在潜意识层面）解读了你对出版社的态度——那对你而言本质上具有危险性。事实确实如此。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: It does mean that action is turned into channels that are not to the best interests of the whole personality. The energies appear concentrated and turn inward, affecting the whole system. They represent offshoots, again not necessarily detrimental in themselves, but only when viewed from the standpoint of the other actions that form the personality framework.
quotes.translation: 这确实意味着行动被导向了不利于整个人格最佳利益的渠道。能量看似集中却转向内里，影响整个系统。它们代表旁支，本身未必有害，但若从构成人格框架的其他行动角度来看则另当别论。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: However, it seems to you that all action in the past is fixed and done, while behavior in the future alone is open to change -- so the word "prediction" assumes future action. Basically, the past is as open to change as the future is. When you are dealing with historic events you believe that no prediction is involved. Personally and as a species, you are convinced that there is a one-line series of finished events behind you.
quotes.translation: 然而在你看来，所有过去的行动都已固定完成，唯有未来的行为尚可改变——因此"预测"一词预设了未来行动。但本质上，过去与未来同样可以改变。当你处理历史事件时，你认为其中不涉及预测。无论作为个体还是物种，你都坚信身后存在一条由已完成事件构成的单线序列。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 741
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: When he sips of it, as you have sipped of it, and as all conscientious human beings sip of it, then indeed the taste is bitter. It is not too farfetched however to add that all, or many, medicines have unfortunately a foul taste, and that the child who sips such a medicine finds it difficult to believe that such a distasteful brew can do him good. Basically, all action Basically there is no evil action. All is unfolding. With the limited perceptions that the ego has itself adopted, the whole is not visible, and it sees what it will see. Within your field, within your moral field, you must indeed strike out against that which appears evil to you.
quotes.translation: 当他啜饮这杯苦酒时——正如你曾啜饮过，正如所有有良知的人类都啜饮过——那滋味确实苦涩。然而，若说所有或多数药物不幸都带有恶臭，而啜饮此药的孩童难以相信这般难喝的药汤能治愈他，这倒也不算牵强附会。从根本上说，所有行动皆无邪恶。一切皆是展开。由于自我所采纳的有限感知，整体不可见，它只看见它愿见之物。在你的领域内，在你的道德领域内，你确实必须对抗那些在你眼中显为邪恶的事物。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The probable system, therefore, is as complicated as the reincarnational one. Now I have told you that all action is simultaneous; therefore, on the one hand, you exist in both systems at once. However, to explain to you that decisions are involved, and to separate these events, I must simplify them to some degree. Put it this way: A portion of the whole self focuses in reincarnational cycles and handles developments there. Another portion focuses in probabilities and handles developments there.
quotes.translation: 因此，或然系统与转世系统同样复杂。我曾告诉过你，所有行动都是同时发生的；因此，一方面，你同时存在于这两个系统中。然而，为了向你说明其中涉及抉择，并区分这些事件，我必须在某种程度上简化它们。这样说吧：整体自性的一部分聚焦于转世循环，处理那里的发展；另一部分聚焦于或然性，处理那里的发展。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: From the "chaotic" bed of your dreams springs your ordered daily organized action. In your reality, the behavior of your consciousness and of your molecules are highly connected. Your type of consciousness presupposes a molecular consciousness, and your kind of consciousness is inherent in molecular consciousness -- inherent within your system, but not basically predictable. Predictability is simply another word for significance. Unpredictability, looking at itself in a variety of different fashions, finds certain portions of itself significant, and forms certain orders, or ordered sequences, about itself. In one of our very early sessions, I told you that you perceive from a vast field only certain data that you find meaningful. That data could only arise from the bed of unpredictability. Only unpredictability can provide the greatest source of probable orders.
quotes.translation: 从你梦境那"混沌"的温床中，生发出你井然有序的日常行动。在你的实相中，你意识的运作与你分子的运作高度关联。你的意识类型预设了分子意识的存在，而你的意识类型本就内在于分子意识之中——内在于你的系统之内，但基本上不可预测。可预测性不过是重要性的另一种说法。不可预测性以各种不同方式审视自身时，发现自身某些部分具有重要性，并围绕自身形成特定秩序或有序序列。在我们早期的一次课程中，我曾告诉你，你从广袤场域中只感知到那些你认为有意义的数据。这些数据只能源于不可预测性的温床。唯有不可预测性才能提供最丰富的或然秩序之源。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 681
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: The full practical implication here should be understood: No course is irrevocably set or beyond change. Within the limited framework of your usual operations, however, so-called predictions3 may be made. They will be workable to some degree. In deeper terms however no action is set beyond alteration. The unknown reality is the source for the known one. If you want to "discover" how things work, then your journey must eventually lead you into the dimensions that lie within the world you know.
quotes.translation: 此处应理解其完整的实际意涵：没有任何路径是绝对固定或无法改变的。然而，在你通常运作的有限框架内，所谓的预测或许可行，并在某种程度上具有实用性。但就更深层的意义而言，没有任何行动是固定不变而无法改变的。未知实相是已知实相的源头。若你想"发现"事物如何运作，你的旅程终将引领你进入你所知世界内在的维度。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 704
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: The action is only apparent or visible in a particular field when it adopts the camouflage coloration of the field in question. Basically the action has not changed. The camouflage distortion is like the effect that water reflections have upon the image that falls across the water.
quotes.translation: 行动只有在特定场域中采用该场域的伪装色彩时，才显得可见或显现。本质上，行动并未改变。这种伪装扭曲犹如水面倒影对投射其上的影像产生的影响。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You cannot probe into the one system without affecting the others. It is basically meaningless to consider the personality as separate from the simultaneous actions of which it is composed. The personality, as you know, has also a reality within the dream universe. It should be obvious that in a most basic and practical manner the personality however is not involved with physical time to any degree. Only a part of the personality, the ego, is so involved. It is obvious of course that the personality system will react to stimuli that seem, to the ego, to be far divorced in time. That is, the personality may react to a stimuli in the present that occurred originally twenty years ago, to the ego's understanding of time.
quotes.translation: 你无法在不影响其他系统的情况下探究单一系统。将人格视为独立于构成它的同时性行动之外，基本上毫无意义。如你所知，人格在梦境宇宙中也具有实相。显然，在最根本且实际的意义上，人格丝毫未涉入物质时间。只有人格的一部分——自我——才涉入其中。当然，人格系统会对那些在自我看来时间上相隔甚远的刺激产生反应。也就是说，以自我对时间的理解，人格可能对当下发生的刺激做出反应，而该刺激最初发生于二十年前。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Its nature becomes known, for the personality, again, is not an object, nor is it an unchanging unit. You have been studying it in the past in a very superficial manner. It should be remembered however that hypnotism is also an action, and as such hypnotism will change the personality to some extent, as all action changes and affects other actions.
quotes.translation: 其本质得以显现，因为人格再次并非一个客体，也非一个不变的单元。过去你们一直以非常肤浅的方式研究它。然而应记住，催眠也是一种行动，因此催眠会在某种程度上改变人格，正如所有行动都会改变并影响其他行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: You see perhaps havoc within the physical field, and this is indeed to be faced and dealt with, and set straight, as aid is given to the victims of a hurricane. But you are familiar only with the results of action as they appear within the physical field, as long as you insist upon viewing your physical universe with the eyes of the ego-self; for the ego-self attempts to cut itself off from that action of which it is a part, and in so attempting it loses contact with this larger reality.
quotes.translation: 你或许在物质领域看到混乱，这确实需要面对、处理并纠正，就像为飓风受害者提供援助一样。但只要你坚持用自我意识的眼睛审视物质宇宙，你就只熟悉行动在物质领域显现的结果；因为自我意识试图将自己与它所属的行动割裂开来，而这样做时，它便失去了与这更大实相的联系。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Obviously, time in terms of continuity, is not an element of importance in the dream universe, and this is one of the reasons for the lack of physical structure. The intuitions and certain inner abilities here have so much more freedom that it is unnecessary for molecules to be used in any imprisoning form or structure. Action is more spontaneous within the dream universe, more fluid. The images appear and disappear much more quickly because value fulfillment is allowed greater reign.
quotes.translation: 显然，以连续性而言的时间，在梦境宇宙中并非重要元素，这也是缺乏物质结构的原因之一。直觉和某些内在能力在此拥有更多自由，以至于无需将分子用于任何禁锢性的形式或结构。行动在梦境宇宙中更为自发、更为流动。影像出现和消失的速度快得多，因为价值完成被赋予了更大的主导权。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: There is no separate outside identity or force, such as "force;" the two words here are being used with different meanings. There is no separate force that causes action. All of these points are extremely important, and if I speak slowly it is to insure words as nearly correct as possible. Action is perceivable in some cases, and not in others.
quotes.translation: 不存在独立于外的身份或力量，例如“力”——此处两个词含义不同。没有独立的力量能引发行动。这些观点都极其重要，若我语速缓慢，是为了确保用词尽可能准确。行动在某些情况下可被感知，在另一些情况下则不能。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Every action changes every other action. We return to our ABCs. Therefore every action in the present affects those actions which you call the past. Ripples from a thrown stone go out in all directions.
quotes.translation: 每个行动都会改变其他所有行动。我们回到最基础的概念。因此当下的每个行动都会影响那些你称之为“过去”的行动。投石激起的涟漪会向四面八方扩散。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Because of its nature ego does not want to adjust. It wants adjustments to be made to it. Because ego is another manifestation of action, it is of course impossible for its aims to be realized. For all its attempts at stability and control, ego itself constantly changes. ego most of all resents and fights against time as you know it, yet ego is to a large extent responsible for your conception of time. Basically, ego fears both the past and the present. It fears the past because it has already lost control of the past. It fears the future because it is not yet in control of it. It seeks continuity of identity, yet it is forced to realize that the "I" of today is hardly the "I" of thirty years ago.
quotes.translation: 由于自身本质，自我不愿调整，它要求万物向它调整。既然自我是行动的另一种显化，其目标自然无法实现。尽管自我竭力追求稳定与控制，它自身却在持续变化。自我最憎恶并抗拒你所认知的时间，然而在很大程度上，正是自我造就了你对时间的概念。从根本上说，自我既恐惧过去也恐惧现在——它恐惧过去是因为已失去对过去的掌控，恐惧未来则因尚未掌控未来。它寻求身份认同的连续性，却被迫意识到今日之“我”与三十年前的“我”已判若两人。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: This is different however from cause and effect for basically a specific action will not give a specific effect, only. Within your system, you only perceive certain actions out of an endless variety of actions. So you take these few as inevitable results of a given cause.
quotes.translation: 然而这与因果律不同，因为基本上特定行动不会只产生特定结果。在你们的系统内，你们只能从无穷无尽的行动中感知到某些行动。于是你们将这些少数行动视为特定原因的必然结果。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: As for your straight lines and curves you realize, I know, that there are really no straight lines and curves, but that they are symbols. Otherwise your dissertation was excellent. Action is solidified, or should I say transfixed, in a painting and yet even in a painting action is never really solidified or transfixed, but continually fluid.
quotes.translation: 至于你们所说的直线与曲线，我知道你明白实际上并不存在真正的直线与曲线，它们只是象征符号。否则你的论述堪称完美。行动在画作中被凝固——或该说被钉住——但即使在画作中，行动也从未真正凝固或钉住，而是持续流动的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Although someone begins reading this material on page one, for example, this does not mean that page four hundred does not already exist. Your outer ego is forced into what could be called successive action, but the inner ego is not so bound. This should make the point more clear. I mentioned earlier that cause and effect operates in various manners, and what seems to be cause and effect is often merely a result of your necessary disposition to view actions in a successive manner.
quotes.translation: 例如，虽然有人从第一页开始阅读这份资料，但这并不意味着第四百页尚未存在。你们的外在自我被迫进入可称为连续行动的状态，但内在自我却不受此限制。这应该能让观点更清晰。我先前提到因果律以多种方式运作，而看似因果的现象，往往只是你们必须将行动视为连续序列的倾向所造成的结果。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Initially this is mental action, an action event. This action event will then affect all other events, spiral inward and outward in all possible dimensions, and may be perceived in these dimensions in quite a different form from its original nature. In each system it can only be perceived according to the camouflage patterns inherent there.
quotes.translation: 起初这是心智行动，一个行动事件。此行动事件随后会影响所有其他事件，在所有可能维度中向内向外螺旋扩展，并可能在这些维度中以与其原始本质截然不同的形式被感知。在每个系统中，它只能根据该系统中固有的伪装模式被感知。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: The action and counteraction is the time trigger. To you this will be almost unbelievable, but on some other planes motion is simultaneous and time unknown. To me time can be manipulated, used at leisure and examined. To me your time is a vehicle, one of the several vehicles by which I can enter your awareness. It is therefore still a reality of some kind to me. Otherwise I could not utilize it in any manner whatsoever. As an example of my good intentions I will end this session.
quotes.translation: 行动与反行动是时间的触发器。对你们而言这几乎难以置信，但在某些层面上运动是同时发生的，时间并不存在。对我而言，时间可以被操控、从容运用并加以审视。对我而言，你们的时间是一种载具，是我进入你们意识所借助的几种载具之一。因此它对我而言仍是某种实相，否则我根本无法以任何方式利用它。作为我善意的证明，我将结束本次课程。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Such actions naturally possess all the characteristics of action in general, and therefore will seek other methods of materialization and expression. An attempt at discipline will be made. The structure will seem, that is the impeding structure will seem, to maintain itself. The whole personality at any given time, because of its own nature and characteristics, has only a given amount of energy available to it in practical terms, though ideally speaking its energy is not limited.
quotes.translation: 这类行动自然具备行动的一般特性，因此会寻求其他物质化与表达的方式。将尝试进行纪律约束。结构似乎会——即阻碍性的结构似乎会——维持自身。整个人格在任何特定时刻，因其自身本性与特质，实际上只能获得特定量的可用能量，尽管理想上其能量是无限的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: We are also helped here by several characteristics of the personality, in that it is forever changing, and its flexibility will be of benefit. We merely want to change the direction in which it moves, or rather the direction in which some of its energy moves. It must be seen by the personality that the impeding action is a hardship on the part of the whole structure, and that this particular portion of the self is not basic to the original personality structure, but only adopted.
quotes.translation: 人格的某些特质在此也对我们有所帮助，因为它永远在变化，其灵活性将带来益处。我们只想改变它移动的方向，或者更确切地说，改变它部分能量移动的方向。人格必须认识到，阻碍行动对整个结构而言是一种负担，而自我的这一特定部分并非原始人格结构的基础，只是后来才被采纳的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: (Slowly at 11:06:) When such opportunities are denied then there are riots, wars, and natural catastrophes. A sense of power is any creature's right. I speak here again of power as the ability to act creatively and with some effectiveness. A dog chained too long often becomes vicious. A man who believes his actions have no value seeks out situations in which he uses his power to act, yet often without worrying about whether the action will have a constructive or negative effect.
quotes.translation: （缓慢地，11:06）当这样的机会被剥夺时，就会发生骚乱、战争和自然灾害。任何生物都有权拥有力量感。我再次谈到力量，即创造性地且有效地行动的能力。一条被长期拴着的狗往往会变得凶猛。一个认为自己的行动毫无价值的人，会寻找情境来运用他行动的力量，却往往不关心这行动会产生建设性还是负面的效果。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In this case, the frightened perceiver knows full well that the terrible events on the screen will not suddenly explode into the living room. When you become caught in frightening physical events, however, it is equally foolhardy to yell or shout or stamp your feet, because that is not where the action is (smiling). Again, you have only to change your station. But often you become so engrossed in your life situation that you do not realize the inappropriateness of your response.
quotes.translation: 在这种情况下，受惊的观看者很清楚，屏幕上可怕的事件不会突然爆炸到客厅里。然而，当你陷入可怕的物理事件时，大喊大叫或跺脚同样鲁莽，因为那不是行动所在之处（微笑）。再说一次，你只需改变你的频道。但通常你过于沉浸在自己的生活情境中，以至于没有意识到你的反应是多么不恰当。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This is an instance of true traveling, or psychic motion. Now this quality that could be said to be a substitute for your space and time, this Quality-depth, represents the perspective in which this sort of psychic traveling or psychic motion, or any psychic action, occurs; and its depth can be understood not in terms of downward action, but perhaps you can comprehend it if you think of a deep trance, for example, as definitely having motion, though the body may be motionless.
quotes.translation: 这是真实旅行或心灵运动的实例。这种可被视为你们时空替代品的品质——品质深度，代表了此类心灵旅行、心灵运动或任何心灵行动发生的视角；其深度不能以向下运动来理解，但若以深度出神为例，或许你能领会——尽管身体静止，它确实具有运动性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: This situation can be serious in varying degrees, according to the impetus and intensity of the original propelling cause behind the impeding action. If the impetus is a powerful one, then the impeding action will be of more serious nature, blocking up large reserves of energy for its own purposes. It obviously becomes part of the personality-psychological structure, the physical structure, the electrical and chemical structure, invading to some extent even the dream universe.
quotes.translation: 根据阻碍行动背后原始推动力的冲劲与强度，此情况可能呈现不同程度的严重性。若冲劲强大，则阻碍行动的性质将更为严重，为达成自身目的而封锁大量能量储备。它显然会成为人格-心理结构、生理结构、电性与化学结构的一部分，甚至在一定程度上侵入梦境宇宙。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The core of the self is obviously aware of all realities to some extent. If portions of the self did not coincide then it would be impossible for the whole personality to ever operate as a unit. Here we simply have spirals, so to speak, of ever moving actions that compose the whole self. But portions of these spirals coincide, and in this analogy the spirals of action not only have those dimensions which you understand, but other dimensions with which you are not yet familiar.
quotes.translation: 自我核心显然在某种程度上知晓所有实相。若自我各部分不相重合，则整个人格绝无可能作为整体运作。此处我们不妨说，构成完整自我的乃是不断运动的行动螺旋。但这些螺旋的部分区域相互重合，在此类比中，行动螺旋不仅具备你们所知的维度，更拥有你们尚未熟悉的其它维度。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: There is no one particular pattern followed by consciousness in its perception of itself as action. Mankind is more familiar with certain patterns and relatively unfamiliar with others. Any action changes itself. Nothing is constant. This rule is not forced upon action from some outside agency, but is simply a part of its own nature.
quotes.translation: 意识在感知自身为行动时，并没有遵循任何特定的模式。人类对某些模式较为熟悉，而对其他模式则相对陌生。任何行动都会改变自身。没有什么是恒常不变的。这一法则并非由外在力量强加于行动，而仅仅是其自身本质的一部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now, Philip. Indeed when I speak of the future I do see possibilities, and speak in terms of trends of activities that may change. This is why, often, our specific dates do not materialize as given, or why events foreseen do not occur as given. For at no time are any events predestined. There should be no such word in your vocabulary, for with every moment you change, and every heartbeat is an action, and every action changes every other action.
quotes.translation: 现在，菲利普。确实，当我谈及未来时，我确实看到各种可能性，并以可能改变的活动趋势来表述。这就是为什么我们给出的具体日期常常未能如期实现，或预见的事件未能按预期发生。因为没有任何事件是预先注定的。你们的词汇中不应存在"宿命"这个词，因为每一刻你都在改变，每一次心跳都是一个行动，而每个行动都会改变其他所有行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Those odd genetic happenings, however, as I have tried to explain, often provide a resiliency and a widening of probabilities that are most necessary for overall genetic balance. Dream actions can indeed--and often do--affect genetic alterations, acting as triggers for altered cellular action. There is a give-and-take between the seemingly separate mental and physical aspects of your lives at every level of experience, and at every level within nature's seeming boundaries.
quotes.translation: 然而，正如我曾试图解释的，那些奇特的基因事件往往提供了必要的弹性和概率的扩展，这对整体基因平衡至关重要。梦中的行动确实能够——而且经常——影响基因改变，作为触发细胞行动改变的扳机。在你们生活的每个经验层面，以及在自然看似界限内的每个层面，看似分离的心智与身体面向之间存在着一种相互给予与接收的关系。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Jane held Session 900 for Chapter 5 of Dreams, in Volume 1, some 20 months ago. In Note 1 for that session I described a most vivid dream experience--one in which, Seth told me in the session itself, I had viewed the many-faceted light of my own being and of the universe. Participating in that event had been our friend, Floyd Waterman. Floyd is an extremely generous and caring individual who has helped us many times over the years; he's the contractor who converted half of our double garage for the hill house into Jane's writing room.2 Jane and I have each shared a number of psychic experiences with him.

quotes.text: I have mentioned before that in its own way the dream universe is as permanent as the physical universe. Basically its structure is somewhat the same, in that it is composed of atoms and molecules. These particles however simply exist within a different perspective. The inner logic is much more consistent within the dream universe, and action is permitted greater freedom in several important respects.
quotes.translation: 我曾提过，梦境宇宙以其自身方式与物质宇宙同样永恒。其基本结构在某种程度上相似，同样由原子与分子构成。然而这些粒子仅存在于不同的视角中。梦境宇宙的内在逻辑更为连贯，且行动在若干重要层面被赋予更大自由。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Another more personal core belief: "My life is worthless. What I do is meaningless." Now a person who holds such an idea will ordinarily not recognize it as an invisible belief. Instead he or she may emotionally feel that life has no meaning, that individual action is meaningless, that death is annihilation; and connected to this will be a conglomeration of subsidiary beliefs that deeply affect the family involved, and all those with whom such a person comes in contact.
quotes.translation: 另一个更个人化的核心信念："我的生命毫无价值。我所做的一切毫无意义。"持有这种想法的人通常不会将其视为无形信念，反而会在情感上感到生命无意义、个体行动无意义、死亡即湮灭；与此相连的是一系列附属信念，深刻影响着相关家庭及所有接触者。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: These are reflected in the physical system, for it is geared, and naturally so, for intense action, and here he functions best. I am speaking now of his ideal performance. When he is working well his rest is in his work. When he demands less of himself and then more, and then less, the physical system, geared for steady highly-focused action, becomes confused; the glandular systems upset, and the nervous system as well. The unused energy ties him up in knots, you see.
quotes.translation: 这些都会在物质系统中得到反映——因其天然适应高强度行动，在此状态下人类表现最佳。此刻我谈论的是其理想表现。当运作良好时，休息即寓于工作之中。若他时而苛求自己，时而放任，又时而严苛，这个本应稳定聚焦高强度行动的物质系统便会陷入混乱；腺体系统与神经系统随之失调。你看，未被使用的能量会将他束缚成结。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: You must remember the material I gave you concerning moment points, and the nature of action. All of that material you see applies here. Again, you merely perceive a small portion of any given action, and when you cease to perceive it then it seems to you that the action itself ceases, and so an artificial boundary is erected.
quotes.translation: 你必须记住我给你的关于"片刻点"和行动本质的资料。你明白所有这些资料在此都适用。再次强调，你只感知到任何给定行动的极小部分，当你停止感知它时，对你而言行动本身似乎也终止了，于是便人为地树立起一道界限。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: The soul can be considered as an electromagnetic energy field, of which you are part. It is a field of concentrated action when you consider it in this light -- a powerhouse of probabilities or probable actions, seeking to be expressed; a grouping of nonphysical consciousnesses that nevertheless knows itself as an identity. Look at it this way: The young woman through whom I speak once stated in a poem, and I quote: "These atoms speak, and call themselves my name."
quotes.translation: 灵魂可被视为一个电磁能量场，你正是其一部分。若以此角度观之，它便是一个浓缩的行动场——一个寻求表达的可能性或可能行动的能量库；一群虽自知为独立身份却仍属非物质意识的集合体。不妨这样看：借我之口说话的年轻女子曾在诗中写道，容我引用："这些原子在说话，并自称为我的名字。"
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You perceive only the most initial elements of such an action. It is as if you threw a ball, and could only follow the ball three inches away in space--then the ball would seem to vanish to you. The action would therefore seem completed. You would think it idiotic to image what happened to the ball when you could see it no longer, for habit would work in such a way that the disappearance of the ball would seem natural and normal, and a part of the nature of things.
quotes.translation: 你只感知到此类行动最初始的元素。这好比掷出一颗球，却只能在三英寸的空间距离内追踪它——随后球便在你眼前消失。于是这行动看似已完成。你会认为想象球消失后的去向是愚蠢的，因为习惯会使球的消失显得自然而然，成为事物本质的一部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: If you will again recall our material on the fifth dimension, you will see how this must be the case. It will be far more simple to see such a distortive effect of one mental action as it occurs in the dream and matter universes. In all such cases the mental action occurs simultaneously in all systems in which it will have a reality; in which it can be used as a reference point in other words.
quotes.translation: 若你再次回想我们关于第五维度的资料，便会明白为何必然如此。观察单一心智行动在梦境与物质宇宙中产生的扭曲效应，将更为简单。在所有这类情形中，心智行动同时发生于所有它将在其中具有实相的系统——换言之，即所有它能被用作参考点的系统。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The continued existence of your physical body is determined by action, although consciously you are not aware of this most of the time. Action may not seem to be going any place. Action, by its nature, while part of every reality necessarily changes that reality and forms from it a new reality. This should be obvious.
quotes.translation: 你物质身体的持续存在由行动决定，尽管你大部分时间并未有意识地察觉这一点。行动看似未必朝向某个方向。行动就其本质而言，虽为每个实相的一部分，却必然改变该实相，并从中形成新的实相。这理当显而易见。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Impeding actions represent actual blockages of energy or of action, dead-end accumulations. In one manner of speaking this does not mean that the action is terminated, however.
quotes.translation: 阻碍行动代表能量或行动的实际阻塞，是死胡同式的累积。然而，从某种角度来说，这并不意味着行动就此终止。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: This is not as farfetched as it might seem. Much seemingly erratic antisocial behavior could be avoided through such dream therapy. I will even say that crimes of the grossest sort could be prevented. The desired but feared actions would not then gather up toward an explosion. The habitual, overly-aggressive or overly-dependent tendencies would not result in habitual aggressive or dependent behavior, for each individual action would be harmlessly expressed.
quotes.translation: 这并不像表面看来那样牵强。许多看似反常的反社会行为本可通过这种梦境疗法避免。我甚至要说，最严重的犯罪行为也能被阻止。那些既渴望又恐惧的行动便不会积聚成爆发。习惯性的过度攻击或过度依赖倾向也不会导致习惯性的攻击或依赖行为，因为每个个体行动都会得到无害的表达。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Groupings of actions of any kind merge into other groupings, both within the physical field and outside of it. All apparent units are merely formed by functions, the functions of action. In this context the ego is also a function of action. There are of course also functions within functions, which should be obvious.
quotes.translation: 任何类型的行动集群都会与其他集群融合，无论是在物质场域之内还是之外。所有看似独立的单位都仅由功能——即行动的功能——所构成。在此脉络下，自我也是行动的一种功能。当然，功能之中还有功能，这应当显而易见。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: But this is the only reality that can be granted to the present. I am speaking now in your terms only, and this point should be clearly understood, for I am simplifying conditions considerably. A change of attitude, a new association, any of innumerable other actions, will automatically set up new electromagnetic connections, and break others. Now part of this we shall explain later, for these changes obviously affect both the future and the past. But the past, again, is continually changed by you, and by every individual. For basically you see, it is not something done and finished with, as is supposed.
quotes.translation: 但这是唯一能被赋予当下的实相。我现在仅以你们的角度而言，这点必须清楚理解，因为我正大幅简化情况。一个态度的转变、一个新的联想、任何其他数不清的行动，都会自动建立新的电磁连接，并打破旧的连接。这部分我们稍后会解释，因为这些改变显然同时影响未来与过去。但过去同样不断被你们、被每个个体所改变。因为从根本上说，你们明白，它并非如人们所认为的那样是已完成且终结之事。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: When action is allowed to flow unimpeded, the cooperation that is necessary to maintain the efficiency of the gestalt is maintained. It is most frequently the error of the ego, who upon many occasions attempts to deny its dependency upon this cooperation, that sets up impediments, and sets up counter suggestions that can be somewhat considered cancerous, in that if it had its way the ego would envelop all other aspects of the whole organism, and run riot.
quotes.translation: 当行动得以无阻地流动时，维持完形效率所需的合作便得以保持。最常是自我的错误——它屡次试图否认对这种合作的依赖——设置了障碍，并提出了可被视为具有癌变性质的反向建议，因为若任其发展，自我将吞噬整个有机体的所有其他面向，并肆意横行。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: There is always the danger in such discussions that effects which are studied separately will appear to be separate in essence. But what we have been discussing in all these topics is, indeed, the nature of action. The personality as you know it is action. As such the personality however is not physically materialized. You cannot hold it in your hand. You can only observe it in motion, for it is never still, and to probe into it yourself is to change it.
quotes.translation: 在此类讨论中始终存在一种危险：被分开研究的效应，在本质上似乎也是分离的。但我们在所有这些主题中所探讨的，实则是行动的本质。你所知的人格即是行动。然而作为行动，人格并非以物质形态具体化。你无法将它握在手中，只能在运动中观察它——因为它永不停歇，而探究它本身就是在改变它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: I do not want you to feel that you are fated to experience certain events, however, for that is not the case. There will be "offshoots" of the events of your own lives, however, that may appear as overlays in your other reincarnational existences. There are certain points where such events are closer to you than others, in which mental associations at any given time may put you in correspondence20 with other events of a similar nature in some future or past incarnation, however. It is truer to say that those similar events are instead time versions of one larger event. As a rule you experience only one time version of any given action. Certainly it is easy to see how a birthday or anniversary, or particular symbol or object, might serve as an associative connection, rousing within you memories of issues or actions that might have happened under similar circumstances in other times.
quotes.translation: 但我不希望你觉得自己注定要经历某些事件，因为事实并非如此。然而，你自身生命的事件会衍生出"旁支"，这些旁支可能作为叠加层出现在你的其他转世存在中。不过，在某些特定节点上，这类事件会比其他时刻更贴近你——在任一特定时刻，心智联想可能使你与未来或过去转世中其他性质相似的事件产生对应。更准确的说法是，那些相似事件实则是某个更大事件的时间版本。通常你只体验任何特定行动的一个时间版本。显然，生日、纪念日或特定符号、物体如何能作为联想连接点，在你心中唤起其他时代相似情境下可能发生的事件或行动的记忆，这是很容易理解的。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Jane doesn't agree with my doubts. As always, she's been letting me put this book together the way I think best--and inevitably that way has followed how we've been trying to understand our joint long-term situation. She innocently accepts my labors as they come out. And that trust always reflects, I'm sure, Seth's own larger view of reality, as I just quoted him from Session 915. Our challenges echo throughout all of our probable realities simultaneously, and through all of them together the largest picture of Jane and myself is presented. In this probable reality we work with what we can pick up from that great whole. We keep trying to learn to ask better questions.

quotes.text: As to your jottings this morning concerning art, very good, I commend you. You are already beginning to think in new and divider concepts. As your paintings are solidified feeling on board or paper, apparent in the physical universe and therefore vulnerable to the laws of that universe, so is the universe itself on your plane physical materialized feeling, only with more dimension than a drawing or a painting. There is however also an extra dimension in a painting than people usually recognize, beside the lines and color and form and content, which are as you said so aptly feeling solidified. You have the action of which you also spoke. First you have the action of the lines, form and content and so forth, caught and solidified, then as an observer looks at a painting feeling is again motion moving out from the painting into the beholder's perception.
quotes.translation: 关于你今早对艺术的随笔，非常好，我赞赏你。你已开始以崭新且更具区分性的概念思考。正如你的画作是固化在画板或纸张上的情感，在物质宇宙中显现并因此受其法则制约，你们物质层面的宇宙本身也是具象化的情感，只是比素描或油画多了维度。然而画作中还存在人们通常未能察觉的额外维度——除了线条、色彩、形式与内容（如你精辟所言是固化的情感）之外，还有你提及的"行动"。首先你捕捉并固化了线条、形式与内容等元素的行动，当观者凝视画作时，情感再次从画中流动而出，进入观者的感知。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: This is actually not as bad an analogy as I first thought, in that oftentimes a particular personality will be like the right arm of the entity as a whole, while another particular personality will have both feet on the ground. To get back however to the original point I wish to make, there is a point beyond which the most suggestible personality will be beyond reach, no matter what the circumstances are. Manifestations of the personality may follow the lines that suggestion commands. This is bad enough, but it only means that the personality has been forced to change its mode of action in the physical world. The personality seems shattered because the actions seem so changed. And here again we have your cause and effect, misapplied. The basic personality, that is the primary personality, has not been changed and will not change except through the personality itself.
quotes.translation: 这个类比其实比我最初设想的更贴切，因为某个特定人格常如整体存有的右臂，而另一个人格则双脚稳踏实地。但回到我原本想强调的重点：无论环境如何，最易受暗示的人格终会触及某个不可逾越的临界点。人格的表层表现可能遵循暗示的指令，这已足够糟糕，但仅意味着人格被迫改变了在物质世界的行动模式。人格看似支离破碎，只因行动变化如此剧烈。这里我们又看到你误用的因果律。基本人格（即主要人格）从未改变，除非通过人格自身，否则绝不会改变。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: It also unites experience. The unifying and the limiting aspects of consecutive moments are absent in inner reality. Time cannot be counted upon to unify action. The series you see does not exist basically. The unifying elements will be those of your own understanding, your own abilities. You are not forced to perceive action as a series of moments within inner reality, therefore.
quotes.translation: 它也统合经验。内在实相中不存在连续时刻的统合性与限制性。时间无法被依赖来统合行动。你看到的序列在根本上并不存在。统合要素将是你自身理解力与能力的产物。因此在内在实相中，你无需被迫将行动感知为一系列时刻。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: （Now came a second image, partly visual, partly impression. I felt the action was continuing and was still there, as though I could "tune in." Saw several men who seemed to wear some kind of uniforms of dark green, with helmets or helmet-shaped hats on. A work crew perhaps, busy at an activity, something like an evacuation or disembarkation. I then saw one digging in something other than snow. I saw this in several successive tries to see it clearly.
quotes.translation: 接着出现了第二个影像，部分视觉、部分感知。我感觉行动仍在持续，且依然存在，仿佛我能"调频"接收。看到几个穿着深绿色制服的人，戴着头盔或盔形帽。可能是一组工作人员，正忙着某项活动，像是撤离或下船。随后我看到有人在挖掘某种非雪的物质。我连续尝试了几次想看清这个画面。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The ego considers itself the self and considers anything outside of its self as being either no self or another such separate identity, and so the individual man is led to believe that telepathy is basically a communication between two or more basically alone, separate and aloof selves. Telepathy does not operate at the level of the ego, although its actions may protrude into the domain of the ego. Telepathy operates within the inner self, within various levels, different levels of the subconscious, where the ideas of separation and limitations of self are not nearly so limiting.
quotes.translation: 自我视自身为"我"，并将自身之外的一切视为非我或另一个独立个体，因此个体人类被引导相信心电感应本质上是两个或多个本质上孤独、分离且疏离的自我之间的沟通。心电感应并不在自我层面运作，尽管其行动可能渗入自我的领域。心电感应运作于内在自我之中，运作于潜意识的各个不同层面，在那里，关于自我分离与限制的观念远没有那么具有局限性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Some systems are far heavier or lighter than your own, though this may not involve weight in the terms with which you are familiar. Probable actions emerge, then, into matter-systems quite as valid as your own, and quite as consistent. You are used to thinking in single-line thoughts, so you think of events that you know as complete things or actions, not realizing that what you perceive is but a fraction of their entire multidimensional existence.
quotes.translation: 有些系统比你们的系统重得多或轻得多，尽管这可能不涉及你们所熟悉的重量概念。因此，可能性的行动会进入与你们系统同样有效、同样一致的物质系统。你们习惯于线性思维，因此将已知的事件视为完整的事物或行动，却未意识到你们所感知的只是其整个多维存在的一小部分。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: No psychic action is static. These secondary personalities cannot be referred to as full selves, yet they can certainly not be set aside as so-called not-selves. They come into some prominence and fulfillment through dreams, and through enticing the main personality at times into the adoption of conscious or unconscious thoughts which would ordinarily not be chosen by the primary self, and therefore at times altering the course of the primary self.
quotes.translation: 没有心灵活动是静止的。这些次人格不能被称为完整的自己，但当然也不能被当作所谓的非自己而置之不理。它们通过梦境获得某种显要性与实现，有时也通过引诱主要人格采纳通常不会被主要自己选择的有意识或无意识思想，从而时常改变主要自己的进程。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: At another meeting some other aspects were made known to you, Philip, of which you may not be consciously aware. A vague but definite connection to Dayton, OH, here. A man with connections with Dayton, OH will be involved with a change in your company. We speak in terms of probabilities. Nevertheless no actions thus far have occurred to drastically change any of the predicted events, and unless actions are so initiated events will occur as given.
quotes.translation: 在另一次会面中，菲利普，你被告知了其他一些你可能并未有意识地察觉的面向。这里与俄亥俄州代顿市存在一种模糊但明确的关联。一个与代顿市有联系的人将参与你公司的变革。我们以或然性的角度来说。然而，迄今为止尚未有任何行动大幅改变任何预测事件，除非行动如此启动，否则事件将如所述般发生。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: When actions from other systems are projected into your own, your own perceptions distort the true nature of the event. To begin with then, you attempt to understand an action that is poorly perceived. You project upon it your own concepts of space and time, and try to interpret the action in their light.
quotes.translation: 当来自其他系统的行动被投射到你们自己的系统中时，你们自身的感知会扭曲事件的真实本质。因此，你们首先试图理解一个被感知不清的行动。你们将自身对空间和时间的概念投射其上，并试图以这些概念为基准来解释该行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: I will at various times use as an example your friend's ulcer, simply because the ulcer represents an excellent example of an impeding action or illness. It must therefore be clearly understood that an impeding illness is a creation of the personality itself. The very effectiveness and nature of the personality, and health of the personality, is dependent upon the manner in which it handles its ability to choose between various kinds of action.
quotes.translation: 我将不时以你朋友的溃疡为例，仅仅因为它代表了阻碍性行动或疾病的绝佳范例。因此必须清楚理解：阻碍性疾病是人格本身的创造。人格的有效性、本质及其健康状态，完全取决于它如何处理在不同行动之间做出选择的能力。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: As Seth suggests, through even a five-minute exercise, in which we sit quietly and look about, we can become aware that the present is the point of power. In his exercise, we gently remind ourselves that we aren't at the mercy of our past beliefs unless we think we are. We have the full freedom to insert new creative goals in our point-of-power exercises. Next, we relax, to give our fresh suggestions time to begin working within us. Next, physically we make a simple gesture or act, no matter how modest, that is in line with our desires for the future. Periodically we repeat the exercise -- but easily, without pressure, confident that we're doing well. Action is thought in physical motion, Seth tells us. ...
quotes.translation: 正如赛斯所建议的，即使通过五分钟的练习——安静坐着环顾四周——我们也能意识到当下即是力量之点。在练习中，我们温和地提醒自己：除非我们自认为受制于过去信念，否则我们并不受其摆布。我们拥有完全的自由，在力量之点练习中注入新的创造性目标。接着放松，让新鲜的建议有时间在我们内在开始运作。然后，在身体层面做出一个简单的姿势或动作——无论多么微不足道——只要符合我们对未来的渴望。定期重复这个练习，但要轻松不费力，确信自己做得很好。赛斯告诉我们，行动是思想在物质运动中的体现……
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 1. From my dream notebook: (See my painting at end of Session 4.)

related_concepts.name_en: The point of power is in the present

quotes.text: The actions in the various fields affect each other. These actions burn almost like twin fires in different worlds, but they illuminate vastly different landscapes. And while their nature is basically the same, it is always the differences that you notice. We have proceeded well for a beginning. I suggest you break, not into pieces; and that Ruburt now snap back to himself.
quotes.translation: 不同领域的行动相互影响。这些行动如同在不同世界中燃烧的双生火焰，却照亮了截然不同的风景。虽然它们的本质基本相同，但你注意到的始终是差异。作为开端，我们进展良好。我建议你们休息——不是支离破碎——而是让鲁柏此刻回归自身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Identities are never constant, as you yourselves are not the same consciously or unconsciously from one moment to another. Every action is a termination, as we discussed earlier. And yet without the termination, identity would cease to exist, for consciousness without action would cease to be conscious.
quotes.translation: 身份认同从不恒定，正如你们自己从一刻到下一刻，无论有意识或无意识，都不相同。每个行动都是一个终结，如我们先前所讨论的。然而没有终结，身份认同就会停止存在，因为没有行动的意识将不再有意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Usually there are a variety of physical actions, not involving killing, that would suffice. As long as you believe that violence must be met with violence you court it and its consequences. On individual terms, your own body and mind become the battleground, as does the physical body of the earth in mass terms. Your material form is alive through natural aggression, the poised, forceful and directed action that is the carrier for creativity.
quotes.translation: 通常有各种不涉及杀戮的物理行动足以解决问题。只要你们相信暴力必须以暴力回应，你们就是在招引暴力及其后果。就个人层面而言，你们的身心成为战场，就集体层面而言，地球的物理身体也是如此。你们的物质形态通过自然的攻击性而存活——这种蓄势待发、强而有力且方向明确的行动，正是创造力的载体。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: It is this that allows the body its physical manifestation. It is this that allows for all change. It can be called on one level instinct, on other levels it is much more. When it operates at its most efficient level suggestion is indeed the inner affirmation. Without the ego we do not have what you call negative suggestions, for when action is left to itself it flows unimpeded, seeking its fulfillment along its numberless unimpeded ways.
quotes.translation: 正是这一点让身体得以物质显化。正是这一点让所有改变成为可能。在一个层面上可称之为本能，在其他层面上它远不止于此。当它在其最高效的层面运作时，暗示确实是内在的肯定。没有自我，我们就没有你们所谓的负面暗示，因为当行动顺其自然时，它会畅通无阻地流动，沿着其无数畅通无阻的途径寻求实现。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: ("It is a universal truth that whatever you impress on your subconscious mind is expressed on the screen of space as condition, experience, and event. Thought is incipient action. The reaction is a response from your subconscious mind which corresponds with the nature of your thought. Your subconscious mind can be likened to the soil which can grow all kinds of seeds, good or bad. Every thought is a cause and every condition an effect."
quotes.translation: "这是一个普遍真理：无论你在潜意识中印下什么，都会在空间的屏幕上表现为状况、经历和事件。思想是初始的行动。反应是你潜意识中的回应，与你思想的本质相对应。你的潜意识可以比作土壤，能生长各种种子，无论好坏。每个思想都是因，每个状况都是果。"
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Energy cannot be retained. It must be discharged. The very attempt to deny an action automatically changes the nature of the action, and also changes the nature of the individual who attempts to deny it. All energy seeks to materialize itself, which is another way of saying that action must act.
quotes.translation: 能量无法被保留。它必须被释放。试图否认一个行动会自动改变该行动的本质，也改变了试图否认它的个体的本质。所有能量都寻求自我显化，换句话说，行动必须行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: It is therefore with the second alternating group of characteristics that most such problems arise. An ego who can, and has at one time or another accepted as part of itself a violent and unruly desire to kill, for example, will not automatically reject the emotion of hatred. He may dislike it, but he will recognize it as a part of himself during whatever period it is assimilated. An ego which once accepted such an idea of violence, and knew it as a possibility of action, such an ego, if he then rejects the conception, can no longer afford, ever, to recognize this once acceptable emotion, for he is only too aware of the action that could have at one time developed.
quotes.translation: 因此，大多数此类问题都源于第二组交替特征。例如，一个自我能够且曾在某个时刻接受过暴力和难以控制的杀戮欲望作为自身的一部分，它不会自动拒绝仇恨的情绪。它可能不喜欢这种情绪，但在它被同化的任何时期，它都会将其视为自身的一部分。一个曾接受过这种暴力观念并知道它可能发展为行动的自我，如果后来拒绝了这个概念，就再也无法承认这种曾经可接受的情绪，因为它太清楚这种情绪曾可能发展成的行动了。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Such understanding simply cannot follow logical lines. The intellect may indeed grasp some of this understanding from the intuitions, but the intellect itself is aware of only minute portions of the whole personality. Again, this is not meant to minimize the value of the intellect. The fact remains that answers sought by a personality can only be found through a traveling within the actions that compose the self. Within our last session, I explained some of the basic psychological heritage that resides within the action makeup of the personality. There is no escaping this heritage, and it is so important that without it the personality system could not be built up.
quotes.translation: 这种理解根本无法遵循逻辑路线。理智或许能从直觉中捕捉到部分理解，但理智本身只能意识到整个人格中极小的一部分。再次强调，这并非要贬低理智的价值。事实依然是，人格所寻求的答案，只能通过深入构成自我的行动内部去探索。在上一节课中，我解释了人格行动构成中蕴含的一些基本心理传承。这种传承无法逃避，而且至关重要，没有它，人格系统就无法建立。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: In dreams also, where no space as you know it exists, you have complete freedom of space. When the ego gives up its hold upon what it considers control of action, then as in dreams almost any action is possible. And when the ego gives up its claim of space in a dream, all space is available.
quotes.translation: 在梦境中，同样不存在你所认知的空间，你拥有完全的空间自由。当自我放弃它对行动的控制权时，就像在梦境中，几乎任何行动都是可能的。而当自我在梦中放弃对空间的占有，所有空间都可供使用。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: For the actions are spontaneous basically, again, and the effects exist so swiftly that it is impossible to say that one occurs before or after the other, or causes another. This could be likened to some gigantic, spontaneous motion that happens very swiftly. When you viewed such a gigantic motion with a slow camera, then you would get the effect that you receive within your system, of a continuous time. Where actually perhaps a sudden explosion had taken place, you would see a slow progression of light and motion.
quotes.translation: 因为行动本质上是自发的，其效应发生得如此迅速，以至于无法说一个发生在另一个之前或之后，或一个导致另一个。这可以比作某种巨大而迅速发生的自发运动。当你用慢速摄像机拍摄这种巨大运动时，就会得到你在系统中感受到的连续时间效果。而实际上，也许发生了一场突然的爆炸，你看到的却是光与运动的缓慢进展。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Remember here, however, that by action we do not necessarily mean motion as you perceive it. Action is the breath of inner vitality, of which all materializations of any kind are composed. It represents, again, the relationship between unexpressed inner vitality and materialized vitality.
quotes.translation: 但请记住，此处所说的行动并非你们所认知的运动。行动是内在生命力的呼吸，所有形式的物质化都由其构成。它再次代表了未表达的内在生命力与物质化生命力之间的关系。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: As each separate identity then seeks to know and experience its other portions then All That Is learns who and what it is. Action never ceases its own exploration of itself. All That Is can never know itself completely, since action must always act and each action creates a new unknown.
quotes.translation: 当每个独立个体试图认识并体验其自身其他部分时，一切万有便得以知晓自身本质。行动从未停止对自身的探索。一切万有永远无法完全认识自己，因为行动必须持续运作，而每个行动都会创造新的未知领域。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: "In these side pockets, memory, so-called, is not so structured. Its ever-present living elements are apparent; and its growth. Its material is ever-fresh. Here the past still happens. Usually we experience it through neurological connections; that's when it seems vivid or alive, but actually it's that way all the time. Past motion and acts still go on, not recurring -- it's hard to explain -- but those past actions are still exploring other probabilities, while our nervous structure focuses us in the one (physical) probable reality we've chosen. To us those other actions seem terminated ... but that's only because usually we can't follow them.
quotes.translation: "在这些侧袋中，所谓的记忆并非如此结构化。其永恒存在的鲜活元素清晰可见；其成长过程亦如是。其材料永远新鲜。在此处，过去仍在发生。通常我们通过神经连接体验它；那时它显得生动鲜活，但实际上它始终如此。过去的运动与行为仍在持续，并非重复——这难以解释——但那些过去的行为仍在探索其他可能性，而我们的神经结构将我们聚焦于所选择的（物质）可能实相中。对我们而言，那些其他行为似乎已经终止……但这仅仅是因为我们通常无法追踪它们。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (FOR SESSION 685)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Mankind is to some intimate degree acquainted with this attempt of action to recreate itself, for human reproduction is here a case in point, each individual attempting to create a replica, the attempt doomed to failure, but the attempt itself resulting in a distortion of the original action; that is, a distortion of the original individuals, and in the creation of a new reality, this process then being repeated indefinitely.
quotes.translation: 人类在某种程度上熟悉这种行动试图再造自身的尝试，因为人类繁衍正是其中的典型例证——每个个体试图创造复制品，这种尝试注定失败，但尝试本身却导致原始行动的扭曲；即对原始个体的扭曲，并在创造新现实的过程中，这个过程将无限重复。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: We are all within action. We are of it. To be outside of it is impossible. Choice however is limitless within it. In whichever direction we focus our abilities and energies, we seem to see new action, but it is merely our focus which has changed; and in changing, has formed new action.
quotes.translation: 我们都在行动之内。我们由行动构成。置身其外是不可能的。然而，在行动之内选择是无限的。无论我们将能力与精力聚焦于哪个方向，似乎都能看到新的行动，但这只是我们的焦点改变了；而焦点改变时，便形成了新的行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now, no psychological structure is easy to describe in words. Simply to explain the nature of personality as it is generally known, all kinds of terms are used: id, subconscious, ego, superego; all of these to differentiate the interweaving actions that make up the physical personality. The dreaming self is just as complicated. So you can say that certain portions of it deal with physical reality, physical manipulation, and plans; some with deeper levels of creativity and achievement that insure physical survival; some with communication, with even more extensive elements of the personality now generally unknown; some with the continuing experience and existence of what you may call the soul or overall individual entity, the true multidimensional self.
quotes.translation: 现在，没有任何心理结构容易用语言描述。仅仅解释普遍认知中的人格本质，就需要使用各种术语：本我、潜意识、自我、超我；所有这些术语都是为了区分构成物质人格的交织行动。做梦的自我同样复杂。因此你可以说，它的某些部分处理物质现实、物质操控与计划；某些部分处理确保物质生存的更深层创造力与成就；某些部分处理沟通，以及人格中目前普遍未知的更广泛元素；某些部分处理你所谓的灵魂或整体个体实相——即真正的多维自体的持续经验与存在。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: At deep levels the cells are always working with probabilities, and comparing probable actions and developments in the light of genetic information. The most intricate behavior is involved and calculations instantly made, for instance, before you can take one step or lift your finger. This does not involve only the predictive behavior of the physical organism alone, however. At these deeper levels the cellular activity includes making predictive judgments about the environment outside of the body. The body obviously does not operate alone, but in relationship with everything about it. When you want to walk across the room, the body must not only operate using hindsight and "prediction" as far as its own behavior is concerned, but it must take into consideration the predictive activity of all of the other elements in that room.
quotes.translation: 在深层层面，细胞始终与概率打交道，并根据遗传信息比较可能的行动与发展。例如，在你迈出一步或抬起手指之前，就已涉及最精微的行为并瞬间完成计算。然而，这并非仅涉及物质有机体的预测行为。在这些更深层次中，细胞活动包括对体外环境做出预测性判断。身体显然并非独立运作，而是与周遭一切保持关联。当你想要穿过房间时，身体不仅需要根据自身行为的后见之明与"预测"来运作，还必须考虑房间内所有其他元素的预测性活动。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Earlier this evening I reminded Jane of the conversation about cells, versus their components, that we'd had because of Seth's material at 9:38 in Session 705. Some of tonight's book work refers to the questions growing out of our talk I think, as does Seth's brief clarification near the end of the session.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: In the dream state, with your body more or less safe and at rest, and without the necessity for precise action, these psychological intrusions become more apparent. Many of your dreams are like the tail end of a comet: Their real life is over, and you see the flash of their disappearance as they strike your own mental atmosphere and explode in a spark of dream images. They are transformed, therefore, as they travel through your own psychological atmosphere. You could not perceive them in your own state -- nor can they maintain their native state as they plunge through the far reaches of the psyche. They fall in patterns, forming themselves naturally into the dream contents that fit the contours of your own mind. The resulting structure of the dream suits your reality and no other: As this intrusive matter falls, plummets, or shifts through the levels of your own psychological atmosphere, it is transformed by the conditions it meets.
quotes.translation: 在梦境状态中，当你的身体大致安全且处于休息状态，无需精确行动时，这些心理侵入变得更为明显。你的许多梦境如同彗星的尾迹：它们的真实生命已然终结，你看到的只是它们撞击你心灵大气时迸发的闪光，在梦意象的火花中消散。因此，当它们穿越你的心理大气时，已被转化。你无法在自己的状态中感知它们——它们也无法在穿越心灵远域时保持原生状态。它们以特定模式坠落，自然形成符合你心灵轮廓的梦境内容。由此产生的梦境结构只契合你的实相，而非其他：当这些侵入性物质坠落、沉降或穿越你心理大气的各个层面时，会被所遇条件转化。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You cannot begin to have a true psychology, again, unless you see the living self in a greater context, with greater motives, purposes and meanings than you now assign to it, or for that matter than you assign to nature and its creatures. You have denied many impulses, or programmed others so that they are allowed expression in only certain forms of action. If any of you do (underlined) still believe in the Freudian or Darwinian selves, then you will be leery about impulses to examine your own consciousness, afraid of what murderous debris might be uncovered. I am not speaking merely in hypothetical terms. For example, a well-intentioned woman was here recently. She worried about her overweight condition, and [was] depressed at what she thought of as her lack of discipline in following diets. In her dismay, she visited a psychologist, who told her that her marriage might somehow be part of the problem. The woman said she never went back. She was afraid that she might discover within herself the buried impulse to kill her husband, or to break up the marriage, but she was sure that her overweight condition hid some unfortunate impulse.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，除非你将活生生的自性置于更广阔的脉络中，赋予其比你现在所赋予的——或就此而言，比你对自然及其造物所赋予的——更宏大的动机、目的与意义，否则你无法开始拥有真正的心理学。你否定了许多冲动，或对它们进行编程，使它们仅能以特定行动形式表达。若你们中任何人（强调）仍相信弗洛伊德或达尔文式的自性，那么你们会对检视自身意识的冲动保持警惕，害怕可能发现什么凶残的残骸。我并非仅以假设性术语谈论。例如，最近有位善意的女士来访。她为自己的超重状况烦恼，并因认为自己缺乏节食纪律而抑郁。在沮丧中，她咨询了一位心理学家，对方告诉她她的婚姻可能也是问题的一部分。这位女士说她再也没回去过。她害怕可能会发现自己内心深处埋藏着杀死丈夫或破坏婚姻的冲动，但她确信自己的超重状况隐藏着某种不幸的冲动。

quotes.text: I am making generalizations here, but each individual case should be looked at in its own light. Such people as a rule, however, have an exaggerated version of the self (pause), so idealized (long pause) that its very existence intimidates practical action. They are afraid of making mistakes, terrified of betraying this sensed inner psychological superior. Usually, such an idealized inner self comes from the acceptance of highly distorted beliefs--again, concerning good and evil. You end up with what can amount to two main inner antagonists: a superior self and a debased self. The qualities considered good are attracted to the superior self as if it were a magnet. The qualities that seem bad (underlined) are in the same fashion attracted to the debased self. Both of them, relatively isolated psychological polarities, hold about equal sway. All other psychological evidences that are ambiguous, or not clearly understood by either side, group together under their own psychological banners. This is a kind of circular rather than linear arrangement, however, psychologically speaking.
quotes.translation: 我在此做的是概括性论述，但每个个案都应就其本身情况来审视。这类人通常对自我持有夸张的版本（停顿），将其理想化到如此程度（长停顿），以至于其存在本身便阻碍了实际行动。他们害怕犯错，恐惧背叛这种感知到的内在心理优越感。通常，这种理想化的内在自我源于对高度扭曲信念的接纳——再次涉及善恶观念。最终你会形成两个主要的内在对立面：一个优越自我与一个卑劣自我。被视为善的品质如同被磁铁吸引般聚向优越自我，而被视为恶的品质（下划线标注）则以同样方式聚向卑劣自我。这两个相对孤立的心灵极性，几乎拥有同等影响力。所有其他模棱两可或未被任何一方清晰理解的心理证据，都会集结在各自的心理旗帜下。然而从心理学角度而言，这是一种循环而非线性的排列方式。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

source_raw.title: At the beginning of the notes for this 920th session I wrote that the session isn't dictation for Dreams. Yet it is, of course, since in it Seth illuminates from still another perspective his concept of value fulfillment.8 The session grew out of the encounter we had yesterday afternoon with an unexpected visitor from out of state. Seth hardly mentions the individual involved, however, but instead goes into the subject of mental illness in more inclusive terms. I see many correlations between this evening's material and that in Session 917 for Chapter 8; there, among other things, Seth had discussed the reasoning mind, the imaginative mind, and schizophrenia. That session had been triggered, at least in part, by a letter Jane had found to be quite upsetting.

quotes.text: Emotions are also, then, actualities within the overall electric system, existing not as representations of feeling, but as definite charged action. I want if possible to get the idea over that this system involves realities that are directly experienced and vivid, and also that in some ways emotions and thoughts are more directly perceived in that field than your physical field. They are more readily apparent, with more force behind them acting directly upon the electric environment, without any intermediary steps; that is, they, emotions and feelings and thoughts, exist immediately within the environment as electrical forces within that system. In your system they need to be translated and given reality by a method of interrelationships. Your field attains its actuality only as a result of the cooperating merger of many other independent systems.
quotes.translation: 因此，情绪也是整体电性系统内的实相，它们并非作为感受的表征存在，而是作为明确的充电行动。若有可能，我希望传达这样的概念：这个系统涉及直接体验且生动鲜活的实相，并且在某些方面，情绪与思想在该场域中比在你们的物质场域更易被直接感知。它们更为明显可见，其背后蕴含的更大力量直接作用于电性环境，无需任何中介步骤；也就是说，情绪、感受与思想作为该系统中的电性力量，直接存在于环境之中。而在你们的系统中，它们需要经由关系互动的方法被转译并赋予实相。你们的场域唯有通过许多其他独立系统的协同融合才能获得其实际性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: For the investigator himself, through his actions, inadvertently brings about, in specific instances, those results for which he looks. The particular experiment may then seem to suggest conditions which are by no means general ones, but which may appear so. In hypnosis the subject is not as much on guard as a subject of an experiment when the subject knows in advance that he will be awakened by the experimenter, when electrodes are attached to the physical organism, when the conditions of the sleep laboratory are substituted for his ordinary nightly environment. It is impossible to study dreams when an attempt is made to isolate the dreamer from his own personality, to treat dreams as if they were physical or mechanical. The only laboratory for a study of dreams is the laboratory of the personality.
quotes.translation: 因为研究者本人通过其行动，在特定案例中会无意间引发他所寻求的那些结果。特定实验可能因此看似呈现出绝非普遍性的条件，但这些条件却可能显得具有普遍性。在催眠状态下，受试者不像实验对象那样保持警惕——当实验对象事先知晓自己会被实验者唤醒，当电极附着于其肉体组织，当睡眠实验室的条件取代其日常夜间环境时。若试图将做梦者与其人格分离，将梦境视为物理或机械现象来研究，则根本不可能研究梦境。研究梦境的唯一实验室，就是人格的实验室。

quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Now certain chemical changes must come about in the physical organism before projection can occur. Were it not for these you would still be imprisoned within the corporeal image. You know that dreaming has definite chemical bases, that chemicals built up during periods of waking existence are released through dreams. Not only are they released but they form a propelling action that allows energy to flow in the opposite direction. As chemical reactions allow the body to utilize energy and form physical materializations, so the excess built up then becomes a propelling force, allowing action to flow in what you would term subjective directions.
quotes.translation: 在出体发生之前，身体组织内必须先产生某些化学变化。若非如此，你仍将被囚禁于肉体形象之中。你知道做梦具有特定的化学基础，清醒时累积的化学物质会通过梦境释放。它们不仅被释放，还会形成一种推进作用，使能量得以朝相反方向流动。正如化学反应让身体得以利用能量并形成物质显化，累积的过剩能量便成为推进力，使行动得以流向你所谓的主观方向。

quotes.text: (Unfortunately it was a display; at least this is our way of looking at it and certainly our way of looking at it must he tin most important one and our attitudes must guide our actions; no one else altitudes. The information in the first part was excellent, to my way of seeing it; but it was gained at the cost of this exhibition. And in order to use the ability it must be disciplined: it could hardly be allowed to run wild. It probably wouldn't in any case certainly mediums who charge for readings and hold regular ones daily, don't go through that all the time. On the other hand the strength of the ability, also determines the amount of discipline to be used, it has to. You cant clam up so tight in fear that you don't give yourself the opportunity to use or develop the ability surely but you don't use it indiscriminately either。The way it was used the other might does frighten me to some degree surely. I'd even go so far as to say that an overly disciplined attitude would lead to a fairly decent and balanced frame of mind. I permission altitude would make me more frightened. Looking back, I see that the situation actually was strictly supervised; if not I do not think I would have done anything. But the fact is: I'm not sure.
quotes.translation: （不幸的是，这成了一场展示；至少这是我们的看法，而我们的看法当然必须是最重要的，我们的态度也必须引导我们的行动；别人的态度则不然。依我之见，第一部分的信息非常出色；但这是以这场展览为代价获得的。要运用这种能力，就必须加以约束：绝不能任其失控。不过，在大多数情况下，那些收费进行解读并定期举行日常活动的灵媒，并不会一直经历这种情况。另一方面，能力的强度也决定了需要施加多少约束，这是必然的。你不能因恐惧而过度封闭，以至于不给自己运用或发展能力的机会，但也不能不加选择地滥用。上周那次使用方式确实让我感到某种程度的恐惧。我甚至可以说，过度约束的态度反而会带来相当得体且平衡的心智状态。若放任自流，我会更加恐惧。回顾过去，我发现当时的情况实际上受到了严格监督；若非如此，我想我不会采取任何行动。但事实是：我并不确定。）

quotes.text: Every thought in one way or another is constructed by you in physical terms. You cannot escape the result of one thought. Every thought is an actuality. It affects every action and it forms your physical environment. As you sit listening to my voice, you are all subconsciously forming the physical environment of your classroom. You are forming the physical chairs. You are forming the blackboard. You are all forming reality, as you know it. Then with your physical senses, you are perceiving that which you have already so created. If you do not like what you see, then who is to blame? For these are your own constructions , formed in faithful replica to your own thoughts. We do not have the time to explain now how this is done, but in the material we have explained it. Certain telepathic connections exist which are what we term root assumptions, of which you are all telepathically aware. Using these you form a physical environment that is cohesive enough so that you can all agree with what you see and feel and smell and touch.
quotes.translation: 每一个念头都以某种方式被你以物质形式构建出来。你无法逃避任何一个念头的后果。每一个念头都是实相。它影响每一个行动，并塑造你的物质环境。当你坐在这里聆听我的声音时，你们都在潜意识中构建着教室的物质环境。你们在构建这些实体椅子。你们在构建这块黑板。你们都在构建你们所认知的现实。然后，你们用物质感官去感知你们已经创造出来的东西。如果你不喜欢你所看到的，那么该怪谁呢？因为这些正是你自己的构建，忠实地复制了你自己的思想。我们现在没有时间解释这是如何做到的，但在之前的资料中我们已经解释过了。存在某些我们称之为根本假设的通感联系，你们所有人都能通过通感感知到这些假设。利用这些假设，你们构建出一个足够凝聚的物质环境，使得你们都能对所看、所感、所闻、所触达成一致。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: The inner self could be called, then, the nucleus, the original point of action from which all the other emanations that form the whole self began. There is here you see no limitation upon the direction in which action may move, nor any limit to the dimensions which action may create.
quotes.translation: 内我或可称为核心，是行动的原点，所有构成完整自我的其他衍生物皆由此生发。此处你可见行动方向毫无限制，行动所能创造的维度亦无界限。

quotes.text: He requires undue amounts of praise and attention from others, since he obviously will get little from himself. In a fashion, to an extent he will refuse to be accountable for his actions--therefore taking them out of the frame of judgment within which other people must operate. He then can avoid putting his "talents and superior abilities" to the test, where he feels he would certainly fail. He half realizes that the superior self and the debased self are both of psychological manufacture. His abilities are not really that grand. His failures are not nearly that disastrous. The belief in these highly contrasting elements of personality keep him in a state of turmoil, however, so that he feels powerless to act in any concerted fashion.
quotes.translation: 他需要他人过度的赞美与关注，因他显然无法从自身获得这些。某种程度上，他会在一定程度上拒绝为自己的行为负责——从而将这些行为置于他人必须遵循的判断框架之外。如此他便能避免让"天赋与卓越才能"接受考验，因为他深知自己必将失败。他隐约意识到，那个优越的自我与卑劣的自我皆是心理产物。他的能力其实没那么出众，他的失败也远非那般灾难性。然而，对这些人格中强烈对比元素的信念使他持续处于混乱状态，以致他感到无力以任何协调一致的方式行动。
