Seth hardly mentions the individual involved, however, but instead goes into the subject of mental illness in more inclusive terms. quotes.text: This again concerns the nature of action, and I will in our next session begin to discuss the emotions as they are related to, and part of, action. Ruburt's momentary exhaustion this evening is a simple, natural result here of ordinary counterthrusts of emotional action and reaction.
quotes.translation: 这再度涉及行动的本质，在下节课中我将开始讨论情绪——它们与行动相关，且是行动的一部分。今晚鲁柏短暂的疲惫，正是情绪行动与反作用力之间普通反冲的自然结果。

quotes.text: If there is, and there is, stupidity and ignorance and idiotic unfounded pride and rationalizations, nevertheless there is also, at least occasionally, good intent and appreciation. I know that you are aware of the dangers involved in projecting your own fears and irritations upon others. There is always and oftentimes a justifiable tendency to feel unappreciated, and therefore to look for chances to prove this lack of appreciation, so that it is often mistakenly projected on remarks or actions of others. Oftentimes such remarks and actions are the result of the other person's own ignorance, and actually not directed at you personally.
quotes.translation: 纵有愚昧无知、荒谬无据的骄傲与合理化解释存在——且确实存在——但至少偶尔也会有善意与欣赏。我知道你明白将自身恐惧与烦躁投射于他人所蕴含的危险。人们总有一种往往看似合理的倾向，即感到不被赏识，因而寻找机会证明这种不被赏识，以至于常将这种感受错误地投射于他人的言论或行为。而此类言论与行为，往往源于对方自身的无知，实际上并非针对你个人。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: "Usual memory is as much a sifting process as it is anything else, in which experience's intensity varies -- sometimes 'alive' neurologically and sometimes not -- just to focus our consciousness in one probable action or series. (As I type I add: We forget anything not pertinent to our selected series of probable actions. The psyche knows its own parts. Seth says so in his books, but we ask the psyche the wrong questions.)
quotes.translation: "寻常记忆本质上既是筛选过程，也是其他事物——其中经验的强度各有不同：有时在神经学层面"鲜活"，有时则不然——这不过是为了将我们的意识聚焦于某条或某系列可能行动。正如我打字时补充：我们会遗忘任何与所选可能行动系列无关的事物。心灵知晓自身的各个部分。赛斯在其著作中如此阐述，但我们却向心灵提出了错误的问题。

quotes.text: It must be thoroughly understood that under no circumstances is the personality a static or motionless construction. It is, instead, a collection of spontaneous actions. Only when it is viewed in this perspective can you begin to understand how it forms its own health. All basic adjustments to the personality, in a basic manner, must come from within, through regroupings of characteristic actions about unifying principles.
quotes.translation: 必须彻底理解：人格绝非静止或僵化的结构。相反，它是自发行动的集合。唯有以此视角审视，你才能开始理解它如何构建自身的健康。所有对人格的根本调整，本质上都必须源自内在，通过围绕统一原则重组特征性行动来实现。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: I do not mean to mislead you. Basic reality is not by nature terrifying. It is however vibrant and direct to the extreme, and you must be prepared before you experience such an ultrasonic action. A direct experience of reality involves complete use of all the inner senses to an integrated cognizance field. A very weak analogy can be found if you imagine at one time hearing the most exciting and moving music imaginable, while simultaneously smelling the strongest but not necessarily unpleasant odor, viewing the most emotionally-charged scene while feeling intense and vivid bodily sensations. In normal life you even turn down outer-sense stimuli for the sake of simplicity, and to enable you to focus upon those stimuli close at hand.
quotes.translation: 我无意误导你们。基本实相的本质并不可怕，但它充满活力且直接到极致，你们在体验这种超音速行动之前必须做好准备。对实相的直接体验涉及完全运用所有内在感官，进入一个整合的认知场。一个非常薄弱的类比是：想象你同时听到最激动人心、最动人的音乐，闻到最强烈但未必难闻的气味，观看最富情感冲击的场景，同时感受强烈而鲜明的身体知觉。在正常生活中，你们甚至为了简化而调低外在感官的刺激，以便专注于手边的刺激。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: In your terms, obviously, atoms do not dream of cats chasing dogs, yet (intently) there are indeed "lapses" from physical focus that are analogous to your dreaming state. Give us a moment ... In those conditions the atoms pursue their own probable activities, and indeed make astounding calculations, bringing into your actuality the necessary probable actions to insure official life forms. But neither are they limited otherwise, for their other probable directions are also actualized. Period. On different levels in the dream state, then, you are also subjectively aware of other probable realities. Your conscious intent is unconsciously brought into the dreaming condition, and that intent helps you sort the data. (See Note 4.)
quotes.translation: 用你们的说法，原子显然不会梦见猫追狗，但（专注地）确实存在与你们做梦状态类似的“脱离”物理焦点。请稍等……在这些条件下，原子追求它们自己的可能活动，并确实进行惊人的计算，将必要的可能行动带入你们的现实，以确保官方生命形式。但它们在其他方面也不受限制，因为它们的其他可能方向也同样被实现了。句号。因此，在梦状态的不同层次上，你们也主观意识到其他可能实相。你们的有意识意图被无意识地带入做梦状态，这个意图帮助你们整理数据。（见注4。）
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Because of its own nature it must act, yet no action can ever complete itself. The sphere would act in as many ways as were open to it, and every action changes that which is acted upon. Therefore each action would create a new reality. In such a manner are all fields activated.
quotes.translation: 由于其自身本质，它必须行动，但没有任何行动能完成自身。这个球体将以其所能的多种方式行动，而每个行动都会改变被作用的对象。因此，每个行动都会创造一个新的实相。所有场域都以这种方式被激活。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: "The idea is that the scientists' system of beliefs is bound to result in some destructive action; that is, the implied attitudes of today's scientists lead them to be less careful of life than they should be, and separate them from nature in a way that leads to some contempt on their parts of individual living things. The leaders of religious cults, like that of Jonestown, overexaggerate grandiose ideals of brotherhood and love, for example (as Seth has mentioned), while often forbidding the natural expression of love on the part of one individual for another -- assaulting family affiliations and so forth. As a result, the idealized love becomes more and more inaccessible, with the growth of more guilt and despair.
quotes.translation: 关键在于科学家的信念体系必然导致某些破坏性行为；也就是说，当今科学家隐含的态度使他们对待生命不如应有的谨慎，并以某种方式与自然分离，导致他们对个体生命产生某种程度的轻蔑。宗教崇拜的领袖们，例如琼斯镇事件中的领袖，过分夸大博爱与手足情谊的崇高理想（如赛斯所言），却常常禁止个体之间自然表达爱意——攻击家庭纽带等等。结果，理想化的爱变得越来越遥不可及，随之而来的是更多的内疚与绝望。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: NOTES: SESSION 845
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: There is a chemical necessity, as I have said, that makes dreaming inevitable. But then these dreams in turn affect the personality in general, and affect the actions of that personality in a physical universe. It goes without saying that telepathy operates within the dreaming state quite as effectively as it operates while the individual is awake. In the waking state it operates subconsciously. But in all times there is no boundary, generally speaking, that exists to separate one psychological unit from another. There are differences between psychological units, and you concentrate upon these differences. Nevertheless one man's dreams affect another's, and that man is in turn affected by the dreams of his neighbor.
quotes.translation: 正如我所说，存在一种化学上的必然性，使得做梦不可避免。但这些梦境反过来又会影响整体人格，并影响该人格在物质宇宙中的行为。不言而喻，心灵感应不仅在清醒状态下有效运作，在梦境状态中同样有效。在清醒状态下，它潜意识地运作。但总的来说，在任何时候，都不存在将不同心理单元分隔开来的界限。心理单元之间存在差异，而你们专注于这些差异。然而，一个人的梦境会影响另一个人的梦境，而那个人又反过来受到邻居梦境的影响。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The ego, then, is as much characterized by those types of action which it habitually denies, as by those types of actions which it habitually accepts. It is not the only organizing aspect of the personality, however. It is simply the organizing aspect of the personality in its dealings with the physical environment. The inner ego is another organizing feature of the personality in its dealings with inner environment.
quotes.translation: 因此，自我既由其习惯性否认的行为类型所塑造，也由其习惯性接受的行为类型所塑造。然而，它并非人格唯一的组织面向。它只是人格在处理物理环境时的组织面向。内在自我则是人格在处理内在环境时的另一个组织面向。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: You must realize that fanatics always deal with grandiose ideals, while at the same time they believe in man's sinful nature, and the individual's lack of power. They cannot trust the expression of the self, for they are convinced of its duplicity. Their ideals then seem even more remote. Fanatics call others to social action. Since they do not believe that the individual is ever effective, their groups are not assemblies of private individuals come reasonably together, pooling individual resources. They are instead congregations of people who are afraid to assert their individuality, who hope to find it in the group, or hope to establish a joint individuality -- and that is an impossibility (emphatically).
quotes.translation: 你必须明白，狂热分子总是处理宏大的理想，同时他们相信人类有罪的本性，以及个人缺乏力量。他们无法信任自我的表达，因为他们确信其虚伪性。他们的理想因此显得更加遥远。狂热分子号召他人参与社会行动。由于他们不相信个人能真正有效，他们的团体并非由个体理性地聚集、整合个人资源而成。相反，他们是那些害怕彰显个性、希望在团体中找到个性、或希望建立共同个性的人群——而这是不可能的（强调地）。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

9:39 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: If the personality handles his dream activities capably, then the problem action finds release. When the ego is too rigid, it will even attempt to censor dreams. When the personality in general is too rigid, freedom of action is not entirely permitted even in the dream state.
quotes.translation: 如果人格能妥善处理其梦境活动，那么问题行动就能得到释放。当自我过于僵化时，它甚至会试图审查梦境。当人格整体过于僵化时，即使在梦境状态中，行动的自由也无法完全被允许。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Someday we shall explain this in mathematical terms. However for now we shall try plain English. Every thought is composed of its own energy, and it has an effect within energy. We are not speaking here now of your tired old cause and effect theory however. Every action changes every other action. Any probability is a reality whether or not it occurs within your own system, and I shall add to that early mention.
quotes.translation: 有一天我们会用数学术语来解释这一点。然而现在，我们将尝试用简单的英语。每个思想都由其自身的能量构成，并在能量中产生影响。不过，我们现在谈论的不是你们那陈旧过时的因果理论。每个行动都会改变其他每个行动。任何可能性都是一个实相，无论它是否发生在你们自己的系统内，我将在早期提及的基础上补充这一点。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Irregardless of our particular interests, I look forward to long conversations with you on less formal topics... like yourself, I am independent. We have many fields of common interests... the personality should always be considered in an elemental way as patterns of action. It is never stable. When you attempt to tamper with various levels you change it. When you crack an egg to discover what is inside you ruin the egg. There are other ways to get inside the egg, and they are not too difficult to do... you have the sort of mind, Dr. Instream, that will be of great benefit to us here. We do not need a hammer to crack the eggshell. I am an egghead, but I do not need a hammer to be cracked.
quotes.translation: 不论我们各自的兴趣为何，我期待与你进行更多非正式话题的长谈……就像你一样，我独立自主。我们有许多共同感兴趣的领域……人格应始终被视为行动模式的基本元素。它从不稳定。当你试图干涉不同层面时，你便改变了它。当你敲开鸡蛋想看看里面是什么时，你毁掉了鸡蛋。还有别的方法可以进入鸡蛋内部，而且并不太难做到……英斯特里姆博士，你拥有那种对我们这里极有帮助的头脑。我们不需要锤子来敲碎蛋壳。我是个书呆子，但我不需要锤子来被敲开。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: It could still do this and retain its own nature, merely by allowing into its awareness the reality of other actions as a part of its self-image. There is for example no basic reason why the subconscious and the ego cannot communicate to a much larger extent than is now usual. Such communication would result in the acceptance of additional energy and action by the ego, and an expansion of the ego's self-image.
quotes.translation: 它仍能保持自身本质，只需将其他行动的现实性作为自我形象的一部分纳入其觉知。例如，潜意识与自我之间没有根本理由不能进行比现在更广泛的沟通。这种沟通将导致自我接纳额外的能量与行动，并扩展自我的自我形象。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: I said that the inner ego reasons, but its reasoning is not restricted to the cause-and-effect limitations that you apply to the reasoning process. The action of the inner ego within the wider sphere of Framework 2 explains many events and seeming coincidences that otherwise seem to make no sense within your world. Many realities within Framework 2 cannot suitably be explained as facts to you in Framework 1, simply because they involve psychological thicknesses that cannot be translated into facts as you think of them. These often appear in the symbolic language of the arts instead, and many of your dreams are translations in which the events of Framework 2 appear in symbolic form.
quotes.translation: 我曾说过内在自我会推理，但其推理不受你们应用于推理过程的因果限制。内在自我在框架二更广阔领域中的行动，解释了你们世界中许多看似毫无意义的事件与巧合。框架二中的许多现实无法作为事实恰当地向你们框架一解释，仅仅因为它们涉及无法转化为你们所认知事实的心理厚度。这些现实常以艺术的象征语言呈现，而你们许多梦境正是框架二事件以象征形式出现的翻译。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Your idea of consequences is derived from this fact. Nevertheless your idea of consequences takes into consideration only one small element of any given action. You perceive, in other words, only that part of an action which is projected into your own physical field, and this element you call the natural consequence of the original act.
quotes.translation: 你对后果的概念源自这个事实。然而，你对后果的概念只考虑了任何特定行动中一个很小的元素。换句话说，你只感知到投射到你自身物理场域的那部分行动，而这个元素你称之为原初行为的自然后果。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now. Regard the inner senses in connection with the nature of action and electromagnetic reality, for the perceptions of the inner senses are themselves action, and as such they change both the perceiver and the thing perceived. You can see then that even this astral image is always in motion and never static, and its condition alters the physical self even as the physical self acts upon the astral image.
quotes.translation: 现在。请结合行动的本质与电磁实相来审视内在感官，因为内在感官的感知本身就是行动，而作为行动，它们既改变了感知者，也改变了被感知之物。那么你可以看出，即使是这个星光体影像也始终在运动中，从未静止，它的状态改变着物质自我，正如物质自我作用于星光体影像一样。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Any perception is action; it changes that upon which it acts, and in so doing it is itself changed. The slightest perception alters every atom within your body.24 This, in turn, sends out its ripples, so that as you know, the most minute action is felt everywhere.
quotes.translation: 任何感知都是行动；它改变其所作用的对象，而在这样做的过程中，它自身也被改变。最细微的感知都会改变你体内的每一个原子。这反过来又会发出它的涟漪，因此如你所知，最微小的行动都能被处处感受到。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

source_raw.title: (However, from Session 14 [for January 8, 1964], there's other material that can be given here, as well as some that makes an interesting note at the end of this appendix.4 First, from my own note at 11:05: "Jane said that Seth was quite pleased with the new voice, and that she now knows what he's thinking sometimes, even though he doesn't relay it to or through her as part of a message."
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: You cannot react physically in the same way to projected or imagined dangers. There seems to be no possible reaction. You are frustrated. You are meant to deal with your immediate, primary experience, and in so doing you take care of your responsibility. You are able to take action in your own experience, and therefore affect others. You do not have to be ignorant of wars in other corners of the world, or close your eyes. But if you allow those experiences to overcloud your present, valid intersection with reality, then you speak and act from a position not your own, and deny the world whatever benefits your own present version of reality might allow you to give.
quotes.translation: 你无法以同样的身体方式对投射或想象中的危险做出反应。似乎没有可能的反应。你感到挫败。你本应处理你直接的、首要的经验，这样做时你便承担起你的责任。你能够在自己的经验中采取行动，从而影响他人。你不必对世界其他角落的战争一无所知，也不必闭上眼睛。但如果你允许那些经验遮蔽你当下与实相的有效交集，那么你便从并非属于你自己的立场说话和行动，并否定了世界可能因你当下版本的实相而得以给予的任何益处。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: As a personality is molded by his exterior circumstances, so is he also molded by the dreams that he creates, and which help to form his interior or psychic environment. To the whole self there is little differentiation made between actions that are of an exterior nature, and actions that are of an interior nature. While the ego makes these distinctions, the basic core of the personality does not do so.
quotes.translation: 正如人格被其外在环境所塑造，他也被自己所创造的梦境所塑造，这些梦境有助于形成他的内在或心灵环境。对整体自体而言，外在性质的行为与内在性质的行为之间几乎没有区别。虽然自我做出这些区分，但人格的基本核心却不会如此。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Our tests must be considered in the light of action, for this is what they are. They involve a more immediate and basic action than physical mobility, and therefore we are concerned with manipulations that are not physical. The associations, personal associations on Ruburt's part, when they are directly connected to test objects in our envelope tests, represent to some degree a step forward on his part.
quotes.translation: 我们的测试必须从行动的角度来考量，因为它们本身就是行动。它们涉及比身体移动更直接、更基本的行动，因此我们关注的是非物理的操作。鲁柏方面的联想，当它们与我们信封测试中的测试对象直接相关时，在某种程度上代表了他个人的进步。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: But action must attempt to materialize itself and fulfill itself completely. It cannot do so, and the result is the formation of many selves, that are a part of action, and formed from action; and therefore each self must continue in the creation of other selves.
quotes.translation: 但行动必须试图将自己具体化并完全实现自身。它无法做到这一点，结果便形成了许多自我，这些自我是行动的一部分，由行动所构成；因此每个自我必须继续创造其他自我。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You could (underlined twice) walk into "yesterday" as well as tomorrow at that point of birth -- if you could walk -- and indeed your perception brings you events both in and out of time sequence. Responses to out-of-time events do not bring the infant recognition, approval, or action, however. It immediately begins to learn to accept certain neurological pulses which bring results, and not others, and so neurological patterns are early learned. This can be a frightening process, though it is accompanied by reassurances. The infant sees, out of context, both present and future without discrimination, and (intently) I am speaking of images physically perceived.
quotes.translation: 在那个出生点上，你能够（双下划线）走入“昨天”如同走入明天——如果你能行走的话——而事实上，你的感知将事件带入你，既在时间序列之内，也在时间序列之外。然而，对时间之外事件的反应并不会给婴儿带来认知、认可或行动。它立即开始学习接受某些能带来结果的神经脉冲，而非其他脉冲，因此神经模式在早期就被习得。这可能是一个令人恐惧的过程，尽管伴随着安慰。婴儿在脱离上下文的情况下，不加区分地同时看到现在和未来，（专注地）我指的是物理感知到的影像。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json
